1	# GSD-T Progress
2	
3	## Project: GSD-T Framework (@tekyzinc/gsd-t)
4	## Status: M44 IN PROGRESS — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)
5	## Date: 2026-04-22
6	## Version: 3.17.10
7	
8	## Current Milestone
9	
10	**M44 Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)** — IN PROGRESS (7 proposed domains, scoped in backlog #14). Target: v3.18.10.
11	
12	**Goal**: Deliver task-level parallelism to **both** execution modes on equal footing, with mode-aware gating math. Unattended mode adds the harder contract: **zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run**.
13	
14	**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
15	- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt.
16	- **[unattended]** Run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and **zero compaction**. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate.
17	
18	**Three pre-existing invariants (apply to BOTH modes)**: file-disjointness proof BEFORE spawn · 100% automatic merges · pre-spawn economics check.
19	
20	**Two delivery layers**: L1 parallel `claude -p` worker spawns (primary lever for both modes) · L2 parallel tasks within one worker (weaker lever, when L1 isn't economic).
21	
22	**Pre-reqs landed**: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`) · Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8`/`f7de324`) · Q2b compact_marker frame + visualizer badge (`8abe4ef`) · Q3 turn→tool join fix (`8f4588b`) · optimization report generator (`b5edff2`) · adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`).
23	
24	**Calibration corpus**: 525 turn rows (`.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl`) + 72 compaction events (`.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`) + per-CW report (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`).
25	
26	**Proposed domains (7)** — full scope in `.gsd-t/backlog.md` #14:
27	- D1 — Generic task-graph reader (parses domain tasks + cross-domain deps → DAG)
28	- D2 — `gsd-t parallel` CLI (subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level parallelism, `--mode` aware)
29	- D3 — Command-file integration (`execute`/`wave`/`quick`/`debug`/`integrate` consume task-graph)
30	- D4 — Dep-graph validation (refuses fan-out when deps unmet)
31	- D5 — File-disjointness prover (walks expected-touch lists, sequential fallback if unprovable)
32	- D6 — Pre-spawn economics + cost estimator (queries token-usage.jsonl for similar-task cost, mode-aware gating)
33	- D7 — Per-CW token attribution + compaction-event integration (`cw_id` tagging + estimator calibration loop)
34	
35	**Success criteria**:
36	- [in-session] `gsd-t-execute` previously taking T min serially completes in ≤ T/2 min with N workers, zero pause/resume prompts
37	- [unattended] M1 → M5 minimum (ideally M1 → M10) runs end-to-end with **zero compaction events** during the autonomous chain
38	
39	**Recommended flow**: `partition` → `discuss` (D1 task-graph schema + D6 estimator algorithm need design) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.18.10.
40	
41	**Previous milestone (M43)** completed 2026-04-21, v3.17.10 tagged. Reference DEFINED block preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/` archive.
42	
43	**Goal**: Make every token attributable to a specific tool / command / domain, and lock the framework to a single rule: **the in-session channel is reserved for human↔Claude dialog. All tool-using work spawns. The visualizer is the watching surface.** No flags, no thresholds, no opt-outs — there is no "in-session mode" for commands to enter.
44	
45	**Rationale (revised 2026-04-21)**: The original framing ("headless when pressure is high, in-session otherwise") is wrong. A circuit breaker only makes sense when the default is "in-session, sometimes headless." Once the default is "always headless, dialog-only stays in-session," there is nothing to trip — the spawn already happened. The dialog channel exists for *thinking together*; the visualizer (`:7433`) exists for *watching the doing*. Conflating them is what produced the bee-poc compaction pathology (agent `b94ca63f`, 3 compactions in one workday). The fix isn't smarter routing — it's a clean separation of channels.
46	
47	**Scope — Part A: Universal Token Attribution**
48	- **D1 per-turn in-session usage capture** — Capture token usage for every turn of the dialog channel (the only thing still running in-session under this model). Hook-based if Claude Code exposes `usage` on `Stop` / `SessionEnd` (probe shipped `f94857b`, branch decision pending); otherwise extend the M42 transcript tee to interactive sessions and parse the same stream-json `result` frame the M40 D4 aggregator already understands. Output: per-turn rows into `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with `turn_id`, `session_id`, `command`, `model`, `usage{...}`. Headless spawns are already covered by M41.
49	- **D2 per-tool attribution** — `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs` joins the events stream (`.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl`, which carries every tool-call request/response with `turn_id`) against the per-turn usage JSONL from D1, then attributes the turn's usage across the turn's tool_calls by output-byte ratio. Ships `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool|command|domain [--since YYYY-MM-DD] [--milestone Mxx] [--format table|json]`. Under the always-headless model this matters *more*, not less, because spawned work is now the dominant cost and operators need to slice it.
50	- **D3 sink unification + backfill** — Define one canonical token-row schema (extending M40 D4 schema v1) and route every producer through it: M36 heartbeat, M40 aggregator, D1 dialog capture, D2 joiner. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` becomes a *regenerated view* (`gsd-t tokens --regenerate-log`), not a hand-maintained markdown table. Backfill the existing 64 `Tokens=0` rows from `.gsd-t/events/*.jsonl` + `.gsd-t/headless-*.log` (M41 D3 backfiller existed but was never run against the historical log).
51	
52	**Scope — Part B: Always-Headless Inversion (revised — channel separation, not threshold flip)**
53	- **D4 always-headless command spawn (no flag, no threshold)** — Strip the "headless when over context band" logic from ~14 command files (`execute`, `wave`, `integrate`, `quick`, `debug`, `verify`, `complete-milestone`, `test-sync`, `scan`, `gap-analysis`, `populate`, `feature`, `project`, `partition`). Every command spawns. Period. **No `--in-session` opt-out, no `--headless` flag, no low-water mark, no context-meter-driven branching at spawn time.** The only thing that runs in the dialog channel is the `/gsd` router itself, which always spawns the actual command. Removes ~3 layers of conditional routing logic and the cognitive load of "which mode is this command in right now."
54	- **D5 dialog-channel meter (router-only, replaces the deleted compaction-pressure circuit breaker)** — Originally scoped as a turn-over-turn circuit breaker that would refuse to start in-session commands when growth predicted `/compact`. Under the new channel model there are no in-session commands to refuse. **D5 collapses to a much smaller utility**: a turn-over-turn growth meter on the router itself (since the router is what stays in-session). When dialog growth predicts `/compact` within N turns, surface a one-line warning so the user can `/clear` or wrap the conversation deliberately. Pure read/warn — never refuses, never reroutes (there's nothing to reroute to). Lives in `bin/runway-estimator.cjs` as a small extension, not a new module.
55	- **D6 transcript viewer as the primary surface (not a hint)** — Promote the M42 `:7433` transcript viewer from "useful for unattended runs" / "URL hint alongside in-session option" to **the** surface. Every spawn prints `view at http://127.0.0.1:7433/transcript/{spawn-id}` because that's where the work lives now. The dashboard auto-launches on first command spawn if not already running (port resolved via the project-hashed default from the multi-project isolation quick `df34eb2`). No `--in-session` print path because there is no `--in-session` mode.
56	
57	**Tradeoff acknowledged**: Spawning even tiny work (a single Read, one Bash) has overhead — orchestrator startup, transcript file, dashboard registration. Worth it for clean channel separation and uniform observability. If overhead bites, the answer is to make the spawn faster (M40-style fast spawn), not to make a threshold smarter.
58	
59	**Out of scope (deferred)**:
60	- Intervene / SIGSTOP-inject (already deferred from M42).
61	- Migrating away from `usage`-bearing streams if Claude Code stops emitting them (separate forensic milestone if the contract breaks).
62	- Auto-`/clear` on dialog meter trip — D5 only warns; the user decides when to wrap the dialog.
63	
64	**Contracts touched**:
65	- `metrics-schema-contract.md` v1 → v2 (adds `turn_id`, `session_id`, `tool_attribution[]`; **removes** `compaction_pressure` from the original v2 sketch — D5 collapses, no schema field needed)
66	- `stream-json-sink-contract.md` v1.1.0 → v1.2.0 (formalize the dialog-channel hook entry-point alongside the existing headless-spawn entry-point)
67	- New: `tool-attribution-contract.md` (output-byte ratio algorithm, tie-breaker rules, missing-data handling)
68	- `headless-default-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v2.0.0 (always-headless rule; **delete** `--in-session` opt-out + low-water mark from the v1 spec; document the channel-separation invariant: dialog = router only, everything else spawns)
69	
70	**Success criteria**:
71	1. Every spawn writes a per-turn row to `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` with usage parsed (never `0`, never `N/A` — `—` only when the upstream envelope itself is missing).
72	2. `gsd-t tool-cost --group-by tool` shows non-zero attribution for at least Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Task across the last 30 days of repo activity (after backfill).
73	3. `.gsd-t/token-log.md` is fully regeneratable from `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` (delete + regenerate produces a byte-identical file modulo timestamp ordering).
74	4. **No command file accepts `--in-session` or `--headless` flags.** Default invocation of any of the 14 listed commands spawns. Grep for `--in-session` in `commands/*.md` returns zero matches after D4.
75	5. Dashboard URL is printed at every spawn; visiting it shows the live transcript without operator action beyond clicking the link.
76	6. D5 dialog meter fires a one-line warning on a synthetic high-growth fixture in the router context; never refuses, never reroutes.
77	
78	**Assessment**: MEDIUM (was COMPLEX — D5 collapse and D4 simplification each removed roughly half their original scope). Recommended flow: `partition` → `discuss` (only D1 hook-vs-tee branch needs discussion now; D5 heuristic is no longer in scope) → `plan` → `execute` (waves) → `integrate` → `verify` → `complete-milestone` → tag v3.17.10.
79	
80	**Previous milestone (M42) retained for reference below** (M42 Live Spawn Transcript Viewer — COMPLETE, v3.16.10 tagged 2026-04-20, 3 domains, 29 M42 tests, 1522/1522 suite).

content.content:
1	# M44 Partition — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)
2	
3	**Status**: PARTITIONED
4	**Date**: 2026-04-22
5	**Target version**: 3.18.10
6	**Domains**: 8 (D1 task-graph-reader · D2 parallel-cli · D3 command-file-integration · D4 depgraph-validation · D5 file-disjointness-prover · D6 pre-spawn-economics · D7 per-cw-attribution · D8 spawn-plan-visibility)
7	**Waves**: 3 (Foundation → Gates → Integration)
8	**Rationale source**: M44 scope 2026-04-22 (mode-aware parallelism; see `.gsd-t/progress.md` M44 "Current Milestone" + backlog #14).
9	
10	## Theme Split
11	
12	- **Layer 1 — Parallel `claude -p` worker spawns**: primary lever for both modes. K workers each with a clean CW. For [unattended], this is the compaction-elimination mechanism — slicing work into smaller per-worker pieces that never approach the CW ceiling. For [in-session], it is primarily a wall-clock reducer. D2 delivers the CLI wrapping M40 orchestrator; D3 wires it into the command files.
13	- **Layer 2 — Parallel tasks within one worker**: weaker lever, bounded by one CW. Used when L1 isn't economic (small tasks, shared context). Same disjointness/economics gates apply at sub-iter granularity. This layer is part of D2's `--mode in-session` path.
14	
15	**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
16	- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt. Silent compaction is acceptable; demanding user attention is not.
17	- **[unattended]** Zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate. Speed is a side-benefit. Supervisor (Node, no CW) orchestrates `claude -p` workers.
18	
19	## Domains
20	
21	### D1 — m44-d1-task-graph-reader
22	**Responsibility**: parse `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` and cross-domain dependency declarations; emit a validated DAG of independently-executable task slices. Mode-agnostic.
23	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d1-task-graph-reader/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
24	
25	### D2 — m44-d2-parallel-cli
26	**Responsibility**: new `gsd-t parallel` subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level (not just domain-level) parallelism. Honors `--mode in-session|unattended` flag (or auto-detects from caller). Both modes. Consumes D1 DAG, D4 dep-gate, D5 disjointness-gate, D6 economics gate.
27	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d2-parallel-cli/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
28	
29	### D3 — m44-d3-command-file-integration
30	**Responsibility**: `gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-wave`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-debug`, `gsd-t-integrate` learn to consume the task-graph via D2 CLI and dispatch parallel workers. Both modes. Depends on D2's stable CLI surface.
31	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d3-command-file-integration/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
32	
33	### D4 — m44-d4-depgraph-validation
34	**Responsibility**: pre-spawn validator that confirms task dependencies are honored before fan-out; refuses to spawn tasks whose dependencies have not completed. Mode-agnostic. Read-only against task state artifacts.
35	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d4-depgraph-validation/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
36	
37	### D5 — m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover
38	**Responsibility**: walk each task's expected-touch list against every other concurrent task's list; prove no shared write targets before parallel spawn. Falls back to sequential when unprovable. Sources: domain `scope.md`, prior commit history for similar tasks, optional explicit `touches:` field on task stubs. Mode-agnostic.
39	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
40	
41	### D6 — m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics
42	**Responsibility**: query `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` for prior-similar-task token cost (matches by command + step + domain); estimate per-worker CW footprint; decide parallel-vs-sequential. Mode-aware: feeds [in-session] orchestrator-CW gate vs [unattended] per-worker-CW gate. Calibrated against the 525-row + 72-event corpus.
43	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
44	
45	### D7 — m44-d7-per-cw-attribution
46	**Responsibility**: ensure every spawn (parallel or sequential) tags its token-usage rows with `cw_id` so the per-CW rollup keeps working post-M44. Wire the existing `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` hook output into the supervisor's "we failed to prevent compaction" signal for [unattended] estimator calibration.
47	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
48	
49	### D8 — m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility
50	**Responsibility**: right-side two-layer task panel in the dashboard / transcript visualizer. Layer 1 = project tasks (milestone, waves). Layer 2 = active spawn's task slice. Done tasks display token cell (in/out/cr/cc/cost). One protocol, three writers (`captureSpawn`, `autoSpawnHeadless`, unattended worker resume Step 0), one reader (dashboard SSE). Pure observability — zero added LLM token cost (writer derives plan from `partition.md` + `tasks.md` already on disk).
51	**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.
52	
53	## Wave Plan
54	
55	### Wave 1 — Foundation
56	
57	| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
58	|--------|----------------|-------|
59	| D1 task-graph-reader | Yes — new library, no shared-write conflicts | Parses tasks.md + cross-domain deps → DAG. D2, D4, D5, D6 all consume the DAG object. Lands first. |
60	| D7 per-cw-attribution | Yes — owns `cw_id` field pass-through in token-capture | Foundation because D6's estimator and the post-spawn calibration loop both require `cw_id`-tagged rows. Touches only `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` (unique owner). |
61	
62	**Gate to Wave 2**: D1 emits a well-formed DAG from a synthetic 2-task fixture; D7 `cw_id` pass-through lands in `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` and rows in `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` include the field.
63	
64	### Wave 2 — Gates (parallel-safe)
65	
66	| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Depends on |
67	|--------|----------------|-----------|
68	| D4 dep-graph validation | Yes — new library, reads DAG + task-state artifacts | D1 (DAG emitter) |
69	| D5 file-disjointness prover | Yes — new library, reads scope.md + git history | D1 (task touch-list field) |
70	| D6 pre-spawn economics estimator | Yes — new library, reads D7-tagged token-usage.jsonl | D1 (DAG), D7 (cw_id-tagged rows for calibration corpus) |
71	
72	All three are pre-spawn safety gates and purely read-only against existing artifacts. None touches command files or the orchestrator's execution path — safe to run in parallel.
73	
74	**Gate to Wave 3**: D4 + D5 gates exercised end-to-end against a synthetic 2-task fixture; D6 estimator produces a decision against a real D7-tagged dataset slice (at minimum: the existing 525-row corpus now enriched with `cw_id`).
75	
76	### Wave 3 — Integration (D2 first, then D3; D8 parallel with both)
77	
78	| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
79	|--------|----------------|-------|
80	| D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI | Runs FIRST in Wave 3 (not parallel with D3) | Wires D1 DAG + D4/D5/D6 gates + M40 orchestrator extensions into the new `parallel` subcommand. Also extends `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` with mode-aware gating math. Must be stable before D3 can call it. |
81	| D3 command-file integration | Runs AFTER D2 lands | Edits `commands/gsd-t-{execute,wave,quick,debug,integrate}.md` to dispatch via `gsd-t parallel`. D3 depends on D2's CLI surface being stable. |
82	| D8 spawn-plan-visibility | Yes — fully parallel with D2 AND D3 | New files (`bin/spawn-plan-*`, `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh`, `.gsd-t/spawns/`) + additive edits to `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md`. Disjoint with D2/D3 owned files. Depends only on `captureSpawn` and `autoSpawnHeadless` chokepoints (already exist pre-M44). |
83	
84	**Sequencing in Wave 3**: D2 must land before D3. D8 may run concurrently with either or both (no shared write targets).
85	
86	## Shared Files & Conflict Map
87	
88	| File | Owner | Notes |
89	|------|-------|-------|
90	| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js` | **D2** (parallel subcommand wiring) | D6 callsite for gating math is via the config module, not inline |
91	| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` | **D2** (mode-aware gating math) + **D6** (estimator callsite) | D6 passes estimator result object into config; D2 owns the config file. Both touch different logical sections — D6 adds an `estimateTaskFootprint()` import, D2 adds the in-session orchestrator-CW headroom block. D6 lands in Wave 2 and leaves a clean extension point; D2 completes the wiring in Wave 3. |
92	| `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` | **D3** only | D3 owns the integration block in all 5 command files |
93	| `commands/gsd-t-wave.md` | **D3** only | |
94	| `commands/gsd-t-quick.md` | **D3** only | |
95	| `commands/gsd-t-debug.md` | **D3** only | |
96	| `commands/gsd-t-integrate.md` | **D3** only | |
97	| `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` | **D7 owns `cw_id` field** + **D8 adds `writeSpawnPlan` call before `spawnFn()`** | Two distinct, non-overlapping additive edits: D7 adds the optional `cw_id` field to the row writer; D8 adds a `writeSpawnPlan({...})` call at the top of `captureSpawn` and `markSpawnEnded({...})` after. Different functions, different code blocks. Sequencing: D7 lands in Wave 1, D8 in Wave 3 — no temporal overlap. |
98	| `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` | **D8** only (Wave 3) | Adds `writeSpawnPlan` call before launching the headless child. New code block, no existing logic touched. |
99	| `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` | **D8** only (Wave 3) | Adds `/api/spawn-plans` endpoint + `spawn-plan-update` SSE channel. Additive — does not change existing endpoints. |
100	| `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` | **D8** only (Wave 3) | Adds right-side two-layer panel + CSS + SSE consumer + token-cell renderer. Additive — does not change existing transcript stream rendering. |

# M44 Partition — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (in-session AND unattended)

**Status**: PARTITIONED
**Date**: 2026-04-22
**Target version**: 3.18.10
**Domains**: 8 (D1 task-graph-reader · D2 parallel-cli · D3 command-file-integration · D4 depgraph-validation · D5 file-disjointness-prover · D6 pre-spawn-economics · D7 per-cw-attribution · D8 spawn-plan-visibility)
**Waves**: 3 (Foundation → Gates → Integration)
**Rationale source**: M44 scope 2026-04-22 (mode-aware parallelism; see `.gsd-t/progress.md` M44 "Current Milestone" + backlog #14).

## Theme Split

- **Layer 1 — Parallel `claude -p` worker spawns**: primary lever for both modes. K workers each with a clean CW. For [unattended], this is the compaction-elimination mechanism — slicing work into smaller per-worker pieces that never approach the CW ceiling. For [in-session], it is primarily a wall-clock reducer. D2 delivers the CLI wrapping M40 orchestrator; D3 wires it into the command files.
- **Layer 2 — Parallel tasks within one worker**: weaker lever, bounded by one CW. Used when L1 isn't economic (small tasks, shared context). Same disjointness/economics gates apply at sub-iter granularity. This layer is part of D2's `--mode in-session` path.

**Mode contracts (NON-NEGOTIABLE)**:
- **[in-session]** Speed + reduce compaction as much as possible. Hard rule: NEVER throw an interactive pause/resume prompt. Silent compaction is acceptable; demanding user attention is not.
- **[unattended]** Zero compaction across an autonomous M1 → M10 run. Per-worker CW headroom is the binding gate. Speed is a side-benefit. Supervisor (Node, no CW) orchestrates `claude -p` workers.

## Domains

### D1 — m44-d1-task-graph-reader
**Responsibility**: parse `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` and cross-domain dependency declarations; emit a validated DAG of independently-executable task slices. Mode-agnostic.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d1-task-graph-reader/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D2 — m44-d2-parallel-cli
**Responsibility**: new `gsd-t parallel` subcommand wrapping M40 orchestrator with task-level (not just domain-level) parallelism. Honors `--mode in-session|unattended` flag (or auto-detects from caller). Both modes. Consumes D1 DAG, D4 dep-gate, D5 disjointness-gate, D6 economics gate.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d2-parallel-cli/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D3 — m44-d3-command-file-integration
**Responsibility**: `gsd-t-execute`, `gsd-t-wave`, `gsd-t-quick`, `gsd-t-debug`, `gsd-t-integrate` learn to consume the task-graph via D2 CLI and dispatch parallel workers. Both modes. Depends on D2's stable CLI surface.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d3-command-file-integration/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D4 — m44-d4-depgraph-validation
**Responsibility**: pre-spawn validator that confirms task dependencies are honored before fan-out; refuses to spawn tasks whose dependencies have not completed. Mode-agnostic. Read-only against task state artifacts.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d4-depgraph-validation/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D5 — m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover
**Responsibility**: walk each task's expected-touch list against every other concurrent task's list; prove no shared write targets before parallel spawn. Falls back to sequential when unprovable. Sources: domain `scope.md`, prior commit history for similar tasks, optional explicit `touches:` field on task stubs. Mode-agnostic.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D6 — m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics
**Responsibility**: query `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` for prior-similar-task token cost (matches by command + step + domain); estimate per-worker CW footprint; decide parallel-vs-sequential. Mode-aware: feeds [in-session] orchestrator-CW gate vs [unattended] per-worker-CW gate. Calibrated against the 525-row + 72-event corpus.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D7 — m44-d7-per-cw-attribution
**Responsibility**: ensure every spawn (parallel or sequential) tags its token-usage rows with `cw_id` so the per-CW rollup keeps working post-M44. Wire the existing `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` hook output into the supervisor's "we failed to prevent compaction" signal for [unattended] estimator calibration.
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

### D8 — m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility
**Responsibility**: right-side two-layer task panel in the dashboard / transcript visualizer. Layer 1 = project tasks (milestone, waves). Layer 2 = active spawn's task slice. Done tasks display token cell (in/out/cr/cc/cost). One protocol, three writers (`captureSpawn`, `autoSpawnHeadless`, unattended worker resume Step 0), one reader (dashboard SSE). Pure observability — zero added LLM token cost (writer derives plan from `partition.md` + `tasks.md` already on disk).
**Full detail**: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/{scope,constraints,tasks}.md`.

## Wave Plan

### Wave 1 — Foundation

| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|
| D1 task-graph-reader | Yes — new library, no shared-write conflicts | Parses tasks.md + cross-domain deps → DAG. D2, D4, D5, D6 all consume the DAG object. Lands first. |
| D7 per-cw-attribution | Yes — owns `cw_id` field pass-through in token-capture | Foundation because D6's estimator and the post-spawn calibration loop both require `cw_id`-tagged rows. Touches only `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` (unique owner). |

**Gate to Wave 2**: D1 emits a well-formed DAG from a synthetic 2-task fixture; D7 `cw_id` pass-through lands in `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` and rows in `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` include the field.

### Wave 2 — Gates (parallel-safe)

| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Depends on |
|--------|----------------|-----------|
| D4 dep-graph validation | Yes — new library, reads DAG + task-state artifacts | D1 (DAG emitter) |
| D5 file-disjointness prover | Yes — new library, reads scope.md + git history | D1 (task touch-list field) |
| D6 pre-spawn economics estimator | Yes — new library, reads D7-tagged token-usage.jsonl | D1 (DAG), D7 (cw_id-tagged rows for calibration corpus) |

All three are pre-spawn safety gates and purely read-only against existing artifacts. None touches command files or the orchestrator's execution path — safe to run in parallel.

**Gate to Wave 3**: D4 + D5 gates exercised end-to-end against a synthetic 2-task fixture; D6 estimator produces a decision against a real D7-tagged dataset slice (at minimum: the existing 525-row corpus now enriched with `cw_id`).

### Wave 3 — Integration (D2 first, then D3; D8 parallel with both)

| Domain | Parallel-safe? | Notes |
|--------|----------------|-------|
| D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI | Runs FIRST in Wave 3 (not parallel with D3) | Wires D1 DAG + D4/D5/D6 gates + M40 orchestrator extensions into the new `parallel` subcommand. Also extends `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` with mode-aware gating math. Must be stable before D3 can call it. |
| D3 command-file integration | Runs AFTER D2 lands | Edits `commands/gsd-t-{execute,wave,quick,debug,integrate}.md` to dispatch via `gsd-t parallel`. D3 depends on D2's CLI surface being stable. |
| D8 spawn-plan-visibility | Yes — fully parallel with D2 AND D3 | New files (`bin/spawn-plan-*`, `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh`, `.gsd-t/spawns/`) + additive edits to `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md`. Disjoint with D2/D3 owned files. Depends only on `captureSpawn` and `autoSpawnHeadless` chokepoints (already exist pre-M44). |

**Sequencing in Wave 3**: D2 must land before D3. D8 may run concurrently with either or both (no shared write targets).

## Shared Files & Conflict Map

| File | Owner | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|
| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator.js` | **D2** (parallel subcommand wiring) | D6 callsite for gating math is via the config module, not inline |
| `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` | **D2** (mode-aware gating math) + **D6** (estimator callsite) | D6 passes estimator result object into config; D2 owns the config file. Both touch different logical sections — D6 adds an `estimateTaskFootprint()` import, D2 adds the in-session orchestrator-CW headroom block. D6 lands in Wave 2 and leaves a clean extension point; D2 completes the wiring in Wave 3. |
| `commands/gsd-t-execute.md` | **D3** only | D3 owns the integration block in all 5 command files |
| `commands/gsd-t-wave.md` | **D3** only | |
| `commands/gsd-t-quick.md` | **D3** only | |
| `commands/gsd-t-debug.md` | **D3** only | |
| `commands/gsd-t-integrate.md` | **D3** only | |
| `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` | **D7 owns `cw_id` field** + **D8 adds `writeSpawnPlan` call before `spawnFn()`** | Two distinct, non-overlapping additive edits: D7 adds the optional `cw_id` field to the row writer; D8 adds a `writeSpawnPlan({...})` call at the top of `captureSpawn` and `markSpawnEnded({...})` after. Different functions, different code blocks. Sequencing: D7 lands in Wave 1, D8 in Wave 3 — no temporal overlap. |
| `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` | **D8** only (Wave 3) | Adds `writeSpawnPlan` call before launching the headless child. New code block, no existing logic touched. |
| `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` | **D8** only (Wave 3) | Adds `/api/spawn-plans` endpoint + `spawn-plan-update` SSE channel. Additive — does not change existing endpoints. |
| `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` | **D8** only (Wave 3) | Adds right-side two-layer panel + CSS + SSE consumer + token-cell renderer. Additive — does not change existing transcript stream rendering. |

content.text: Let me check which M44 domains have incomplete tasks and which wave is active.

input.command:
for d in m44-d1-task-graph-reader m44-d2-parallel-cli m44-d3-command-file-integration m44-d4-depgraph-validation m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics m44-d7-per-cw-attribution m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility; do
  echo "=== $d ==="
  if [ -f ".gsd-t/domains/$d/tasks.md" ]; then
    grep -E "^\- \[" ".gsd-t/domains/$d/tasks.md" | head -20
  else
    echo "(no tasks.md)"
  fi
done

for d in m44-d1-task-graph-reader m44-d2-parallel-cli m44-d3-command-file-integration m44-d4-depgraph-validation m44-d5-file-disjointness-prover m44-d6-pre-spawn-economics m44-d7-per-cw-attribution; do
  echo "=== $d ==="
  if [ -f ".gsd-t/domains/$d/tasks.md" ]; then
    wc -l ".gsd-t/domains/$d/tasks.md"
    head -30 ".gsd-t/domains/$d/tasks.md"
  fi
done

- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit 3d6a809)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D1-T5 (D1 complete), M44-D4-T4 (D4 complete), M44-D5-T4 (D5 complete), M44-D6-T5 (D6 complete), M44-D7-T5 (D7 complete)
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs` file exists, exports `runParallel({projectDir, mode, milestone, domain, dryRun})`
  - `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` extended with stubs for `computeInSessionHeadroom` and `computeUnattendedGate` (implementation in T2)
  - `gsd-t parallel --help` prints usage without crashing
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs` (new), `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` (modified), `bin/gsd-t.js` (add `parallel` subcommand routing)

### M44-D2-T2 — Mode-aware gating math implementation
- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit 3ca41ff)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D2-T1
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `computeInSessionHeadroom({ctxPct, workerCount, summarySize})` returns `{ok, reducedCount}` — `ok=true` if `ctxPct + workerCount × summarySize ≤ 85`, else suggests reduced count until N=1
  - `computeUnattendedGate({estimatedCwPct, threshold=60})` returns `{ok, split}` — if `estimatedCwPct > threshold`, `split=true` and the caller must slice the task into multiple iters
  - [in-session] path: `runParallel` calls `computeInSessionHeadroom` before fan-out; if reduced, logs the reduction decision to `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` as a `parallelism_reduced` event
  - [unattended] path: `runParallel` calls D6 estimator + `computeUnattendedGate` before spawning; if split required, emits a `task_split` event and spawns multiple iters
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs`, `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs`

### M44-D2-T3 — `--dry-run` output + end-to-end gate wiring
- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit deff6a6)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D2-T2
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `gsd-t parallel --dry-run` prints: proposed worker plan table (task id | domain | estimated CW% | disjoint? | deps ok? | decision), total worker count, mode used
  - Three gates wired: D4 dep-validation, D5 disjointness prover, D6 economics all called in sequence; any gate veto logs a `gate_veto` event and falls back to sequential for the affected task pair
  - `--mode in-session` and `--mode unattended` explicitly tested against the synthetic 2-task fixture

# Wave Join Contract — v1.1.0

**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator (v1.0.0) · M44 — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (v1.1.0)
**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core (v1.0.0) · m44-d2-parallel-cli (v1.1.0 addendum)
**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume, m44-d2-parallel-cli, m44-d3-command-file-integration

## Purpose
Defines the parallelism and ordering semantics of orchestrator waves: how tasks are grouped, how many run at once, when a wave completes, and what happens on failure.

## Wave Definition
- A wave is a set of tasks sharing the same `wave: N` field in `.gsd-t/domains/{domain}/tasks.md`.
- Wave numbers are non-negative integers. Wave 0 runs first, wave 1 second, etc.
- Waves with no tasks are skipped silently.

## Within-Wave Parallelism
- All tasks in wave N start concurrently via `Promise.all`, capped at `maxParallel` (default: adaptive — `floor(freemem / 2GB)` clamped to floor 3 / ceiling 15 per Team Mode §15).
- If a wave has more tasks than `maxParallel`, the orchestrator uses a simple worker pool: first `maxParallel` start, next starts when any exits.
- No task-level priority within a wave. Tasks are independent by contract (partition asserts this).

## Between-Wave Ordering
- Strict barrier: wave N+1 never starts until every task in wave N is DONE.
- If any task in wave N is FAILED after retry policy is exhausted, the orchestrator halts. Wave N+1 never starts.
- `state.json` records the current wave index; wave completion atomically advances it.

## Failure Semantics
| Event | Behavior |
|-------|----------|
| Task in wave N fails, retry eligible | Fresh worker respawns; other workers in wave N keep running |
| Task in wave N fails second time | Send SIGTERM to all siblings in wave N, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Log `[wave_halt] wave={N} failed_task={id}`. Exit non-zero. |
| Orchestrator itself receives SIGINT | Send SIGTERM to all workers, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Mark wave "interrupted" in state.json. Exit 130. |
| Worker timeout (270 s default) | Task fails with `worker_exited_via_timeout`; retry per D3 policy |

## Recovery After Halt
- On next `gsd-t orchestrate --resume`, D6's recovery algorithm reads state.json + D4 JSONL and decides whether to retry the failed wave or give up (operator-decided via flag).

## Observability
- Wave start: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "start", taskIds: [...]}` synthetic frame.
- Wave end: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "done"|"failed", ts, durationMs}`.
- These are task-boundary-adjacent frames (see stream-json-sink-contract.md).

## Configuration
```json
// .gsd-t/orchestrator.config.json (optional)
{
  "maxParallel": 3,
  "workerTimeoutMs": 270000,
  "retryOnFail": true,
  "haltOnSecondFail": true
}
```

CLI flags override config file; env overrides CLI. Precedence: default < config file < CLI < env.

## Mode-Aware Gating Math (M44 D2 — v1.1.0)

M44 layers task-level parallelism on top of the M40 wave model. The new
`gsd-t parallel` subcommand (owner: m44-d2-parallel-cli) applies a
three-gate sequence (D4 depgraph → D5 disjointness → D6 economics)
followed by a mode-aware headroom / split gate BEFORE any fan-out.

 ## Purpose

@@ -51,6 +51,104 @@ Defines the parallelism and ordering semantics of orchestrator waves: how tasks
 
 CLI flags override config file; env overrides CLI. Precedence: default < config file < CLI < env.
 
+## Mode-Aware Gating Math (M44 D2 — v1.1.0)
+
+M44 layers task-level parallelism on top of the M40 wave model. The new
+`gsd-t parallel` subcommand (owner: m44-d2-parallel-cli) applies a
+three-gate sequence (D4 depgraph → D5 disjointness → D6 economics)
+followed by a mode-aware headroom / split gate BEFORE any fan-out.
+
+Both thresholds are fixed constants, exported from
+`bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs`:
+
+| Constant | Value | Purpose |
+|----------|-------|---------|
+| `IN_SESSION_CW_CEILING_PCT` | 85 | in-session orchestrator-CW ceiling |
+| `UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT` | 60 | unattended per-worker CW ceiling |
+| `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_SIZE_PCT` | 4 | heuristic per-worker summary envelope |
+
+### [in-session] `computeInSessionHeadroom({ctxPct, workerCount, summarySize})`
+
+Returns `{ok, reducedCount}`.
+
+- `ok=true, reducedCount=N` iff `ctxPct + N × summarySize ≤ 85` for the
+  requested worker count N.
+- If the inequality fails, reduce N and recompute. Final floor is N=1.
+- **Never returns `ok=false`.** Constraint (m44-d2 constraints.md §Mode
+  Awareness): in-session mode MUST NEVER throw a pause/resume prompt.
+  Sequential (N=1) is always feasible because the summary envelope is
+  spent post-worker, not pre-worker.
+- When `reducedCount < workerCount`, the caller MUST emit a
+  `parallelism_reduced` event (schema below).
+
+### [unattended] `computeUnattendedGate({estimatedCwPct, threshold=60})`
+
+Returns `{ok, split}`.
+
+- `ok=true, split=false` iff `estimatedCwPct ≤ threshold`.
+- `ok=false, split=true` otherwise — caller MUST slice the task into
+  multiple `claude -p` iters (per-worker CW headroom is the binding
+  gate for unattended; breaching it would cause mid-run compaction,
+  which violates the non-negotiable "zero compaction" contract).
+- Actual task slicing is the orchestrator's responsibility. The gating
+  function only signals; it emits a `task_split` event and the caller
+  schedules the split.
+
+### Event Schemas
+
+All three event types are appended JSONL to
+`.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` (day-rotated). Event-log failures are
+best-effort and must never break the caller's control flow.
+
+**`parallelism_reduced`** — in-session only:
+```json
+{ "type": "parallelism_reduced",
+  "original_count": 5,
+  "reduced_count": 3,
+  "reason": "in_session_headroom",
+  "ts": "2026-04-23T14:00:00.000Z" }
+```
+
+**`task_split`** — unattended only:

+`.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` (day-rotated). Event-log failures are
+best-effort and must never break the caller's control flow.
+
+**`parallelism_reduced`** — in-session only:
+```json
+{ "type": "parallelism_reduced",
+  "original_count": 5,
+  "reduced_count": 3,
+  "reason": "in_session_headroom",
+  "ts": "2026-04-23T14:00:00.000Z" }
+```
+
+**`task_split`** — unattended only:

file.content:
'use strict';

/**
 * GSD-T Spawn Plan Status Updater (M44 D8 T2)
 *
 * Pure module — patches spawn-plan files written by spawn-plan-writer.cjs.
 * Called by the post-commit git hook and by `captureSpawn` on completion.
 *
 * Contract: .gsd-t/contracts/spawn-plan-contract.md v1.0.0
 *
 * Hard rules:
 *   - Atomic rewrites only (temp file + rename)
 *   - No-op on unknown spawnId or unknown taskId — observability is best-effort
 *   - Never throw for missing files; callers rely on silent-fail behavior
 */

const fs = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const SPAWNS_SUBDIR = path.join('.gsd-t', 'spawns');

/**
 * Patch `{spawnId}.json`: find the task with the given id and flip its
 * status to `done`, recording the commit SHA and (optional) token
 * attribution. If the next pending task exists, promote it to
 * `in_progress` so the single-active-task invariant is preserved.
 *
 * @param {object} opts
 * @param {string} opts.spawnId
 * @param {string} opts.taskId

content.content:
# Contract: Spawn Plan

**Version**: 1.0.0
**Owner**: M44 D8 — spawn-plan-visibility
**Status**: Active
**Date**: 2026-04-23

## Purpose

Every GSD-T spawn (interactive subagent, detached headless child, unattended
worker iteration) writes a plan file that answers exactly one question:

> "Of the tasks that were supposed to happen in this spawn, which are done,
> which are in flight, which are pending?"

The dashboard reader (`/api/spawn-plans` + `spawn-plan-update` SSE channel)
and the transcript right-side panel render this plan live; a post-commit
git hook flips task status as commits land; a companion module closes the
plan on spawn exit.

Pure observability. **Zero added LLM token cost.** Derivation is a
deterministic projection of `.gsd-t/partition.md` + `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md`.

## File Layout

```
.gsd-t/spawns/
├── .gitkeep
└── {spawnId}.json     — one file per spawn; active = `endedAt === null`
```

1	# Wave Join Contract — v1.0.0
2	
3	**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator
4	**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core
5	**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume
6	
7	## Purpose
8	Defines the parallelism and ordering semantics of orchestrator waves: how tasks are grouped, how many run at once, when a wave completes, and what happens on failure.
9	
10	## Wave Definition
11	- A wave is a set of tasks sharing the same `wave: N` field in `.gsd-t/domains/{domain}/tasks.md`.
12	- Wave numbers are non-negative integers. Wave 0 runs first, wave 1 second, etc.
13	- Waves with no tasks are skipped silently.
14	
15	## Within-Wave Parallelism
16	- All tasks in wave N start concurrently via `Promise.all`, capped at `maxParallel` (default: adaptive — `floor(freemem / 2GB)` clamped to floor 3 / ceiling 15 per Team Mode §15).
17	- If a wave has more tasks than `maxParallel`, the orchestrator uses a simple worker pool: first `maxParallel` start, next starts when any exits.
18	- No task-level priority within a wave. Tasks are independent by contract (partition asserts this).
19	
20	## Between-Wave Ordering
21	- Strict barrier: wave N+1 never starts until every task in wave N is DONE.
22	- If any task in wave N is FAILED after retry policy is exhausted, the orchestrator halts. Wave N+1 never starts.
23	- `state.json` records the current wave index; wave completion atomically advances it.
24	
25	## Failure Semantics
26	| Event | Behavior |
27	|-------|----------|
28	| Task in wave N fails, retry eligible | Fresh worker respawns; other workers in wave N keep running |
29	| Task in wave N fails second time | Send SIGTERM to all siblings in wave N, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Log `[wave_halt] wave={N} failed_task={id}`. Exit non-zero. |
30	| Orchestrator itself receives SIGINT | Send SIGTERM to all workers, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Mark wave "interrupted" in state.json. Exit 130. |
31	| Worker timeout (270 s default) | Task fails with `worker_exited_via_timeout`; retry per D3 policy |
32	
33	## Recovery After Halt
34	- On next `gsd-t orchestrate --resume`, D6's recovery algorithm reads state.json + D4 JSONL and decides whether to retry the failed wave or give up (operator-decided via flag).
35	
36	## Observability
37	- Wave start: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "start", taskIds: [...]}` synthetic frame.
38	- Wave end: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "done"|"failed", ts, durationMs}`.
39	- These are task-boundary-adjacent frames (see stream-json-sink-contract.md).
40	
41	## Configuration
42	```json
43	// .gsd-t/orchestrator.config.json (optional)
44	{
45	  "maxParallel": 3,
46	  "workerTimeoutMs": 270000,
47	  "retryOnFail": true,
48	  "haltOnSecondFail": true
49	}
50	```
51	
52	CLI flags override config file; env overrides CLI. Precedence: default < config file < CLI < env.
53	
54	## Versioning
55	- Bump major for changes to the strict-barrier semantics (e.g., speculative next-wave).
56	- Bump minor for new frame types or config keys.
57
(Bash completed with no output)

# Wave Join Contract — v1.1.0

**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator (v1.0.0); M44 — Task-Level Parallelism (v1.1.0 addendum)
**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core (v1.0); m44-d2-parallel-cli (v1.1 addendum)
**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume, m44-d2-parallel-cli

content.content:
1	# Wave Join Contract — v1.1.0
2	
3	**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator (v1.0.0) · M44 — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (v1.1.0)
4	**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core (v1.0.0) · m44-d2-parallel-cli (v1.1.0 addendum)
5	**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume, m44-d2-parallel-cli, m44-d3-command-file-integration
6	
7	## Purpose
8	Defines the parallelism and ordering semantics of orchestrator waves: how tasks are grouped, how many run at once, when a wave completes, and what happens on failure.
9	
10	## Wave Definition
11	- A wave is a set of tasks sharing the same `wave: N` field in `.gsd-t/domains/{domain}/tasks.md`.
12	- Wave numbers are non-negative integers. Wave 0 runs first, wave 1 second, etc.
13	- Waves with no tasks are skipped silently.
14	
15	## Within-Wave Parallelism
16	- All tasks in wave N start concurrently via `Promise.all`, capped at `maxParallel` (default: adaptive — `floor(freemem / 2GB)` clamped to floor 3 / ceiling 15 per Team Mode §15).
17	- If a wave has more tasks than `maxParallel`, the orchestrator uses a simple worker pool: first `maxParallel` start, next starts when any exits.
18	- No task-level priority within a wave. Tasks are independent by contract (partition asserts this).
19	
20	## Between-Wave Ordering
21	- Strict barrier: wave N+1 never starts until every task in wave N is DONE.
22	- If any task in wave N is FAILED after retry policy is exhausted, the orchestrator halts. Wave N+1 never starts.
23	- `state.json` records the current wave index; wave completion atomically advances it.
24	
25	## Failure Semantics
26	| Event | Behavior |
27	|-------|----------|
28	| Task in wave N fails, retry eligible | Fresh worker respawns; other workers in wave N keep running |
29	| Task in wave N fails second time | Send SIGTERM to all siblings in wave N, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Log `[wave_halt] wave={N} failed_task={id}`. Exit non-zero. |
30	| Orchestrator itself receives SIGINT | Send SIGTERM to all workers, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Mark wave "interrupted" in state.json. Exit 130. |
31	| Worker timeout (270 s default) | Task fails with `worker_exited_via_timeout`; retry per D3 policy |
32	
33	## Recovery After Halt
34	- On next `gsd-t orchestrate --resume`, D6's recovery algorithm reads state.json + D4 JSONL and decides whether to retry the failed wave or give up (operator-decided via flag).
35	
36	## Observability
37	- Wave start: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "start", taskIds: [...]}` synthetic frame.
38	- Wave end: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "done"|"failed", ts, durationMs}`.
39	- These are task-boundary-adjacent frames (see stream-json-sink-contract.md).
40	
41	## Configuration
42	```json
43	// .gsd-t/orchestrator.config.json (optional)
44	{
45	  "maxParallel": 3,
46	  "workerTimeoutMs": 270000,
47	  "retryOnFail": true,
48	  "haltOnSecondFail": true
49	}
50	```
51	
52	CLI flags override config file; env overrides CLI. Precedence: default < config file < CLI < env.
53	
54	## Versioning
55	- Bump major for changes to the strict-barrier semantics (e.g., speculative next-wave).
56	- Bump minor for new frame types or config keys.
57

input.prompt:
You are the worker subagent for GSD-T milestone **M44 domain D8 (m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility)**. Your job: finish tasks T2 through T7 for this domain and commit each as a separate atomic commit. Follow GSD-T conventions strictly.

PROJECT DIR: /Users/david/projects/GSD-T
CWD: use /Users/david/projects/GSD-T as cwd for all shell commands

=== CURRENT STATE (already done by prior workers, do NOT redo) ===
- T1 done (commit eed80bb) — `bin/spawn-plan-writer.cjs` (204 lines) + `.gsd-t/contracts/spawn-plan-contract.md` (v1.0.0, 146 lines) + `.gsd-t/spawns/.gitkeep`
- The following files are ALREADY PARTIALLY WRITTEN (uncommitted, untracked) and appear to satisfy T2 — verify before committing:
  - `bin/spawn-plan-status-updater.cjs` (292 lines) — exports `markTaskDone`, `markSpawnEnded`, `listActivePlans`, `sumTokensForTask`
  - `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` (86 lines) — extracts `[M\d+-D\d+-T\d+]` from commit msg, calls updater for each active plan
- `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` has an UNCOMMITTED diff (25 lines added) — this is T3's first chokepoint already inlined (calls `writeSpawnPlan` before `spawnFn`). VERIFY the diff is complete per T3 acceptance; if `markSpawnEnded` isn't also called, T3 is NOT yet complete.

=== CONTRACT REFERENCE ===
Read `.gsd-t/contracts/spawn-plan-contract.md` first — it defines the plan JSON shape, status vocabulary, and writer/reader responsibilities. Stick to v1.0.0.

=== T2 — commit existing updater + hook ===
Acceptance (from `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/tasks.md`):
- Module exporting `markTaskDone({spawnId, taskId, commit, tokens?, projectDir})` + `markSpawnEnded({spawnId, endedReason, projectDir})`
- Hook script greps commit msg for `[M\d+-D\d+-T\d+]` matches, scans active spawn plans, looks up token attribution from `.gsd-t/token-log.md` for the task within the spawn's time window, calls updater with `{taskId, commit, tokens}`
- Token attribution lookup: parse token-log rows where Task column matches and Datetime-start >= spawn.startedAt; sum in/out/cr/cc/cost_usd; null if no matches
- Hook template `templates/hooks/post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` for `gsd-t init` to install

Steps:
1. Inspect `bin/spawn-plan-status-updater.cjs` and `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh`. Confirm exports and hook logic match acceptance. If `sumTokensForTask` isn't implemented, add it. If token attribution logic is missing, add it. The hook must parse `.gsd-t/token-log.md` pipe-separated rows, find ones matching task id within the spawn's time window, sum tokens.
2. Create `templates/hooks/post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` — this is the template that `gsd-t init` would copy into the project's `.git/hooks/` directory. It should be identical to `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` but may have a brief template header noting it's auto-installed.
3. If anything is missing, fill in.
4. Stage: `bin/spawn-plan-status-updater.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh`, `templates/hooks/post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` (new), `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/tasks.md` (mark T2 done with placeholder SHA or backfill after).
5. Commit `[M44-D8-T2] spawn-plan status-updater + post-commit hook + template` with a 2-line body explaining the hook greps commit message for task ids, looks up tokens from token-log.md, patches active plans.
6. Record the commit SHA, edit tasks.md to replace the placeholder with the real SHA (small follow-up edit OR include real SHA after committing — whichever you find cleanest).

=== T3 — writer integration in 3 chokepoints ===
Acceptance:
- `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` `captureSpawn()` calls `writeSpawnPlan` before `await spawnFn()` AND calls `markSpawnEnded` after (both success + error paths)
- `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` `autoSpawnHeadless()` calls `writeSpawnPlan` before spawn
- `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` Step 0 (under `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1`) calls `writeSpawnPlan` once at iteration start

Steps:
1. Read the current `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` diff. Confirm `writeSpawnPlan` is called before `spawnFn`. Now ADD a `markSpawnEnded` call in BOTH the success and the error paths. Wrap in try/catch so a status-updater failure never blocks the main spawn flow. Use the `endedReason` value `'completed'` for success, `'failed'` for error, matching the contract's vocabulary.
2. Modify `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` — find the function `autoSpawnHeadless` (or equivalent). Before the `spawn('claude', ...)` call, invoke `writeSpawnPlan({spawnId, kind: 'headless-detached', milestone, projectDir})`. Derive `spawnId` from whatever id the function already uses; if none, generate one with `Date.now()` + random suffix. Wrap in try/catch. NOTE: this is a pure additive edit. Do NOT remove or reorder existing code. Read the file first to find the right insertion point.
3. Modify `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` Step 0 (or Step 0.1 if that's the worker-iteration branch under `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1`). Add a prose block — NOT executable JS — that instructs the worker to call `writeSpawnPlan` once per iteration. This is documentation for the agent running resume; it should be a short block of text telling the agent to `node -e "require('./bin/spawn-plan-writer.cjs').writeSpawnPlan({spawnId: process.env.GSD_T_SPAWN_ID || ('worker-' + Date.now()), kind: 'unattended-worker', milestone: 'M44', projectDir: '.'})"` (or equivalent). Make it additive; don't remove any existing prose.
4. Stage: the three modified files + tasks.md.
5. Commit `[M44-D8-T3] writer integration — captureSpawn, autoSpawnHeadless, unattended resume`.

=== T4 — plan derivation helper ===
Acceptance:
- `bin/spawn-plan-derive.cjs` exporting `derivePlanFromPartition({projectDir, milestone, currentIter})`
- Reads `.gsd-t/partition.md` + `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md`
- Returns `{wave, domains, tasks}` for the current incomplete-tasks slice
- Used by all three writer chokepoints

Steps:
1. Write `bin/spawn-plan-derive.cjs` — a pure module. It should:
   - Parse `.gsd-t/partition.md` to extract current milestone, wave, domains
   - For each domain, read `.gsd-t/domains/{domain}/tasks.md` and collect incomplete tasks `- [ ]` in the active wave
   - Return `{wave, domains, tasks}` where tasks is an array of `{id, domain, status: 'pending', title}`
2. Integrate with the three writer chokepoints — update `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` and `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` to call `derivePlanFromPartition` if `opts.tasks` isn't provided. The resume.md prose should also reference the helper.
3. Stage: `bin/spawn-plan-derive.cjs`, any chokepoint updates, tasks.md.
4. Commit `[M44-D8-T4] plan derivation helper + chokepoint wiring`.

=== T5 — dashboard server endpoint + SSE channel ===
Acceptance:
- `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` adds `GET /api/spawn-plans` returning array of plan files where `endedAt === null`
- Adds `spawn-plan-update` SSE channel; `fs.watch` on `.gsd-t/spawns/*.json` emits `{spawnId, plan}` on change

Steps:
1. Read `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`. Find where existing endpoints are registered. Add a `GET /api/spawn-plans` handler that reads `.gsd-t/spawns/*.json`, filters to `endedAt === null`, returns JSON array.
2. Add SSE channel. Find the existing SSE setup (the dashboard already has SSE for other events). Add an `fs.watch` on `.gsd-t/spawns/` directory — on change, read the plan file, push `{type: 'spawn-plan-update', spawnId, plan}` to connected SSE clients.
3. Additive only — do not alter existing endpoints or SSE channels.
4. Stage + commit `[M44-D8-T5] dashboard spawn-plans endpoint + SSE channel`.

=== T6 — transcript renderer right-side panel ===
Acceptance:
- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` adds `<aside class="spawn-panel">` with two `<section>` (Layer 1 project, Layer 2 active spawn)
- CSS: right-side fixed panel, scrollable, dim when no spawn; token cell right-aligned, monospace, dim color
- JS: SSE consumer for `spawn-plan-update`, initial GET to `/api/spawn-plans`, render functions for both layers, status icon mapping (`☐ ◐ ✓`)
- Token cell renderer: `fmtTokens({in, out, cr, cc, cost_usd})` → `in=12.5k out=1.7k $0.42` with k-suffix above 1000; `—` when null
- Cumulative totals: Layer 1 milestone header sums all done-task tokens; Layer 2 spawn header sums spawn's done-task tokens

Steps:
1. Read `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`. Append the panel HTML, CSS, and JS. Keep edits at the bottom of each section (style block, body, script block) to minimize conflict with future transcript changes.
2. Additive only.
3. Stage + commit `[M44-D8-T6] transcript renderer spawn panel + token cells`.

=== T7 — tests + doc ripple ===
Acceptance:
- `test/m44-d8-spawn-plan-writer.test.js`
- `test/m44-d8-spawn-plan-status-updater.test.js` (covers tokens field write)
- `test/m44-d8-post-commit-hook.test.js` (covers token-log attribution lookup)
- `test/m44-d8-dashboard-spawn-plans-endpoint.test.js`
- `test/m44-d8-transcript-renderer-panel.test.js` (covers fmtTokens helper + cumulative totals)
- `docs/architecture.md` — Observability subsection
- `.gsd-t/progress.md` — Decision Log entry
- `commands/gsd-t-help.md` — note panel + token attribution

Steps:
1. Write the 5 test files. Use Node's built-in `node:test` framework. Each test file must pass `node --test <file>`.
2. For the transcript renderer test: since the HTML has inline JS, either extract `fmtTokens` into a small helper file `scripts/spawn-plan-fmt-tokens.cjs` and require it in the HTML via a module script OR test via string inspection of the HTML. The former is cleaner and testable — prefer it.
3. Run `npm test` — full suite must pass.
4. **CRITICAL — DO NOT DIRECTLY EDIT `docs/architecture.md` OR `.gsd-t/progress.md`**. Those are shared with the parallel D2 subagent. INSTEAD, write a single Decision Log entry line to a holding file `.gsd-t/.m44-d8-doc-ripple-pending.md` with the exact content you would add to each of: architecture.md (one subsection), progress.md (one Decision Log line). The coordinator will merge these into the shared files after both D2 and D8 finish.
5. You MAY directly edit `commands/gsd-t-help.md` — that file is not contested.
6. Stage: 5 test files + `commands/gsd-t-help.md` + `.gsd-t/.m44-d8-doc-ripple-pending.md` + tasks.md + any new helpers.
7. Commit `[M44-D8-T7] spawn-plan tests + help ripple + pending doc-ripple handoff`.

=== CONSTRAINTS (non-negotiable) ===
- Zero external runtime deps. Only Node built-ins.
- Never skip hooks on commit. If pre-commit hook fails, fix the underlying issue and create a NEW commit (do NOT --amend to mask).
- Do NOT touch `docs/architecture.md` or `.gsd-t/progress.md` directly — use the handoff file.
- Do NOT touch anything outside the D8 file list (notably: do NOT touch `docs/requirements.md`, `GSD-T-README.md`, `README.md`, `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs`, `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs`, `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md`, or `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js` — those are D2's).
- Do NOT run destructive git commands.
- Do NOT push.
- If you hit a blocker, stop and report. Do not invent scope.
- Use absolute paths in tool calls.

=== REPORT BACK ===
When done, return a SHORT report (under 250 words) with:
- Per-task commit SHAs (T2, T3, T4, T5, T6, T7)
- `npm test` result (X/Y pass)
- Full paths of files you wrote/modified per task (so coordinator can verify disjointness with D2)
- Contents of `.gsd-t/.m44-d8-doc-ripple-pending.md` (the handoff) — include the exact text so the coordinator can merge
- Any blockers encountered

+++ b/.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md
@@ -1,8 +1,8 @@
-# Wave Join Contract — v1.0.0
+# Wave Join Contract — v1.1.0
 
-**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator
-**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core
-**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume
+**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator (v1.0.0) · M44 — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (v1.1.0)
+**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core (v1.0.0) · m44-d2-parallel-cli (v1.1.0 addendum)
+**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume, m44-d2-parallel-cli, m44-d3-command-file-integration
 
 ## Purpose
 Defines the parallelism and ordering semantics of orchestrator waves: how tasks are grouped, how many run at once, when a wave completes, and what happens on failure.

1	# Contract: Spawn Plan
2	
3	**Version**: 1.0.0
4	**Owner**: M44 D8 — spawn-plan-visibility
5	**Status**: Active
6	**Date**: 2026-04-23
7	
8	## Purpose
9	
10	Every GSD-T spawn (interactive subagent, detached headless child, unattended
11	worker iteration) writes a plan file that answers exactly one question:
12	
13	> "Of the tasks that were supposed to happen in this spawn, which are done,
14	> which are in flight, which are pending?"
15	
16	The dashboard reader (`/api/spawn-plans` + `spawn-plan-update` SSE channel)
17	and the transcript right-side panel render this plan live; a post-commit
18	git hook flips task status as commits land; a companion module closes the
19	plan on spawn exit.
20	
21	Pure observability. **Zero added LLM token cost.** Derivation is a
22	deterministic projection of `.gsd-t/partition.md` + `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md`.
23	
24	## File Layout
25	
26	```
27	.gsd-t/spawns/
28	├── .gitkeep
29	└── {spawnId}.json     — one file per spawn; active = `endedAt === null`
30	```
31	
32	`spawnId` is filesystem-safe: `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,200}$`.
33	
34	## Schema
35	
36	```json
37	{
38	  "schemaVersion": 1,
39	  "spawnId": "gsd-t-execute-2026-04-23-00-10-28",
40	  "kind": "unattended-worker | headless-detached | in-session-subagent",
41	  "startedAt": "2026-04-23T00:10:28.000Z",
42	  "endedAt": null,
43	  "milestone": "M44",
44	  "wave": "wave-3",
45	  "domains": ["m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility"],
46	  "tasks": [
47	    {
48	      "id": "M44-D8-T1",
49	      "title": "spawn-plan-writer module",
50	      "status": "done",
51	      "commit": "5c3844a",
52	      "tokens": { "in": 12483, "out": 1742, "cr": 308, "cc": 134046, "cost_usd": 0.42 }
53	    },
54	    {
55	      "id": "M44-D8-T2",
56	      "title": "post-commit hook",
57	      "status": "in_progress",
58	      "tokens": null
59	    },
60	    {
61	      "id": "M44-D8-T3",
62	      "title": "writer integration at 3 chokepoints",
63	      "status": "pending",
64	      "tokens": null
65	    }
66	  ],
67	  "endedReason": null,
68	  "note": null
69	}
70	```
71	
72	### Status states
73	
74	`pending | in_progress | done`. Only ONE task per spawn may be `in_progress`
75	at a time.
76	
77	### Tokens field
78	
79	- `null` while pending or in_progress
80	- `null` when done without a matching token-log row (rare; renders as `—`)
81	- `{in, out, cr, cc, cost_usd}` when done WITH attribution match
82	
83	Populated by the post-commit hook by parsing `.gsd-t/token-log.md` rows
84	where the `Task` column matches the task id AND `Datetime-start >=
85	spawn.startedAt`; summed across matches.
86	
87	### `note` field
88	
89	When the writer cannot derive a plan (no partition, malformed tasks.md),
90	the file is still written with `tasks: []` and `note: "no-partition"`.
91	This is the "no active spawn plan" state the panel renders.
92	
93	## Writers (3)
94	
95	1. `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` — `captureSpawn()` calls `writeSpawnPlan`
96	   before `await spawnFn()`. Wrapped in try/catch: plan-write failure must
97	   NOT block the spawn.
98	2. `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` — `autoSpawnHeadless()` calls
99	   `writeSpawnPlan` before the detached child launches. Same try/catch.
100	3. `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` Step 0 — under `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1`,
101	   every worker iteration calls `writeSpawnPlan` at iteration start.
102	
103	## Status Updater
104	
105	- `bin/spawn-plan-status-updater.cjs`
106	  - `markTaskDone({spawnId, taskId, commit, tokens?, projectDir})`
107	  - `markSpawnEnded({spawnId, endedReason, projectDir})`
108	- `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` — git post-commit hook.
109	  Scans commit message for all `[M\d+-D\d+-T\d+]` task ids; for each
110	  active spawn plan, calls `markTaskDone` with token attribution from
111	  `.gsd-t/token-log.md`. Silent-fail: always exits 0.
112	
113	## Reader
114	
115	- `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`
116	  - `GET /api/spawn-plans` — array of plan files where `endedAt === null`
117	  - SSE channel `spawn-plan-update` — `fs.watch` on `.gsd-t/spawns/*.json`
118	    emits `{spawnId, plan}` on every change
119	- `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` — right-side `<aside class="spawn-panel">`
120	  with Layer 1 (project) + Layer 2 (active spawn). Renders status icons
121	  (`☐` pending, `◐` in_progress, `✓` done) and token cells (`in=12.5k
122	  out=1.7k $0.42` or `—`).
123	
124	## Invariants
125	
126	1. **Writer derives, never decides.** No LLM calls, no prompts.
127	2. **Spawn must launch even if writer fails.** Try/catch in every caller.
128	3. **Atomic writes only** (temp file + rename).
129	4. **Post-commit hook is silent-fail.** Always `exit 0`.
130	5. **No transcript-derivation fallback.** Missing plan file → "no active
131	   spawn plan." Never reconstruct from transcript heuristics.
132	6. **Only ONE task `in_progress` per spawn** at any time.
133	7. **Reader is additive.** `/api/spawn-plans` is new; existing endpoints
134	   unchanged.
135	
136	## Consumers
137	
138	- Dashboard server + transcript renderer (right-side panel)
139	- Future: `gsd-t-status`, `gsd-t-health` may consume plan files for
140	  at-a-glance spawn status summaries.
141	
142	## Versioning
143	
144	`schemaVersion` on every plan. Minor-compatible field additions bump the
145	patch (1.0.0 → 1.0.1). Any field rename or semantics change bumps the
146	minor (1.0.0 → 1.1.0). Removals bump the major.
147

## Versioning

input.new_string:
CLI flags override config file; env overrides CLI. Precedence: default < config file < CLI < env.

## Mode-Aware Gating Math (v1.1.0 — M44 D2)

M44 introduces task-level parallelism via the `gsd-t parallel` CLI. Unlike the v1.0 wave model (coarse, wave-barrier orchestration), v1.1 adds a fine-grained, mode-aware gate layered on top of the existing D4/D5/D6 gates.

### Modes

| Mode | Selection | Gating Strategy | Compaction-Elimination |
|------|-----------|-----------------|------------------------|
| **in-session** | default; or `--mode in-session` | orchestrator-CW headroom check | no |
| **unattended** | `--mode unattended`; or `GSD_T_UNATTENDED=1` | per-worker-CW estimator + split | yes |

Explicit `--mode` always overrides env auto-detection.

### The Three Upstream Gates (apply in both modes)

Before the mode-aware final gate, every candidate task pair passes through:

1. **D4 — Dependency graph validation** — every task's declared `Dependencies:` must be DONE or the task is vetoed (`gate_veto`, gate="deps") and falls back to sequential.
2. **D5 — File-disjointness prover** — two tasks' `Touches:` file sets must have empty intersection, else vetoed (`gate_veto`, gate="disjointness") and both tasks fall back to sequential.
3. **D6 — Economics estimator** — per-task estimated CW% must be reasonable relative to available budget; veto → `gate_veto`, gate="economics".

A `gate_veto` event is emitted to `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` for every fallback; the task is removed from the parallel batch but NOT dropped — it executes sequentially afterward.

### Final Gate — in-session path

```
computeInSessionHeadroom({ ctxPct, workerCount, summarySize = 4 })
  → { ok: true, reducedCount: N }
```

- **Ceiling**: `IN_SESSION_CW_CEILING_PCT = 85` — orchestrator's own context window must not exceed 85% after fan-out.
- **Math**: `projected = ctxPct + workerCount × summarySize`. If `projected ≤ 85`, `reducedCount = workerCount`.
- **Reduction**: if projected > 85, iteratively reduce `workerCount` by 1 until projected ≤ 85 OR the floor `N=1` is reached.
- **Never refuses**: floor is `N=1`. Under all pressure, at least one worker runs. Result: `ok=true` always; `reducedCount` is the truth.
- **Emits**: `parallelism_reduced` event with `{ requestedCount, reducedCount, ctxPct, reason: "in_session_headroom" }` when `reducedCount < requestedCount`.
- **Default `summarySize`**: `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_SIZE_PCT = 4` (% of the orchestrator CW consumed per returned worker summary).

### Final Gate — unattended path

```
computeUnattendedGate({ estimatedCwPct, threshold = UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT })
  → { ok: boolean, split: boolean }
```

- **Threshold**: `UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT = 60` — per-worker (not orchestrator) CW budget.
- **Math**: if `estimatedCwPct > threshold`, `ok=false` and `split=true` — the caller MUST slice the task into multiple iters. Else `ok=true, split=false`.
- **Boundary**: exactly 60 is OK (`≤`, not `<`).
- **Compaction-elimination**: by holding each worker's own CW below 60%, unattended workers finish their iter without a mid-run `/compact`. This is the unattended-mode guarantee the in-session mode cannot offer.
- **Emits**: `task_split` event with `{ taskId, estimatedCwPct, iters }` when `split=true`.

### Fallback Summary

| Gate | Veto Signal | Fallback |
|------|-------------|----------|
| D4 deps | `gate_veto` deps | Task moves to sequential tail |
| D5 disjointness | `gate_veto` disjointness (both tasks) | Both tasks sequential |
| D6 economics | `gate_veto` economics | Task sequential |
| In-session headroom | `parallelism_reduced` | Reduce `N` to fit; floor `N=1` |
| Unattended per-worker CW | `task_split` | Split into iters before spawn |

### Exported Constants (bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs)

```
IN_SESSION_CW_CEILING_PCT   = 85   // orchestrator-CW ceiling after fan-out
UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT = 60  // per-worker-CW pre-spawn threshold
DEFAULT_SUMMARY_SIZE_PCT     = 4   // per-worker summary cost in in-session mode
```

Consumers MUST import from `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs`; these are the single source of truth.

## Versioning
- Bump major for changes to the strict-barrier semantics (e.g., speculative next-wave).
- Bump minor for new frame types, config keys, or gating-math additions (v1.1.0 adds the Mode-Aware Gating Math section).

1	# Wave Join Contract — v1.1.0
2	
3	**Milestone**: M40 — External Task Orchestrator (v1.0.0) · M44 — Cross-Domain & Cross-Task Parallelism (v1.1.0)
4	**Owner**: d1-orchestrator-core (v1.0.0) · m44-d2-parallel-cli (v1.1.0 addendum)
5	**Consumers**: d0-speed-benchmark, d6-recovery-and-resume, m44-d2-parallel-cli, m44-d3-command-file-integration
6	
7	## Purpose
8	Defines the parallelism and ordering semantics of orchestrator waves: how tasks are grouped, how many run at once, when a wave completes, and what happens on failure.
9	
10	## Wave Definition
11	- A wave is a set of tasks sharing the same `wave: N` field in `.gsd-t/domains/{domain}/tasks.md`.
12	- Wave numbers are non-negative integers. Wave 0 runs first, wave 1 second, etc.
13	- Waves with no tasks are skipped silently.
14	
15	## Within-Wave Parallelism
16	- All tasks in wave N start concurrently via `Promise.all`, capped at `maxParallel` (default: adaptive — `floor(freemem / 2GB)` clamped to floor 3 / ceiling 15 per Team Mode §15).
17	- If a wave has more tasks than `maxParallel`, the orchestrator uses a simple worker pool: first `maxParallel` start, next starts when any exits.
18	- No task-level priority within a wave. Tasks are independent by contract (partition asserts this).
19	
20	## Between-Wave Ordering
21	- Strict barrier: wave N+1 never starts until every task in wave N is DONE.
22	- If any task in wave N is FAILED after retry policy is exhausted, the orchestrator halts. Wave N+1 never starts.
23	- `state.json` records the current wave index; wave completion atomically advances it.
24	
25	## Failure Semantics
26	| Event | Behavior |
27	|-------|----------|
28	| Task in wave N fails, retry eligible | Fresh worker respawns; other workers in wave N keep running |
29	| Task in wave N fails second time | Send SIGTERM to all siblings in wave N, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Log `[wave_halt] wave={N} failed_task={id}`. Exit non-zero. |
30	| Orchestrator itself receives SIGINT | Send SIGTERM to all workers, wait 5 s, SIGKILL. Mark wave "interrupted" in state.json. Exit 130. |
31	| Worker timeout (270 s default) | Task fails with `worker_exited_via_timeout`; retry per D3 policy |
32	
33	## Recovery After Halt
34	- On next `gsd-t orchestrate --resume`, D6's recovery algorithm reads state.json + D4 JSONL and decides whether to retry the failed wave or give up (operator-decided via flag).
35	
36	## Observability
37	- Wave start: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "start", taskIds: [...]}` synthetic frame.
38	- Wave end: emit `{type: "wave-boundary", wave: N, state: "done"|"failed", ts, durationMs}`.
39	- These are task-boundary-adjacent frames (see stream-json-sink-contract.md).
40	
41	## Configuration
42	```json
43	// .gsd-t/orchestrator.config.json (optional)
44	{
45	  "maxParallel": 3,
46	  "workerTimeoutMs": 270000,
47	  "retryOnFail": true,
48	  "haltOnSecondFail": true
49	}
50	```
51	
52	CLI flags override config file; env overrides CLI. Precedence: default < config file < CLI < env.
53	
54	## Mode-Aware Gating Math (M44 D2 — v1.1.0)
55	
56	M44 layers task-level parallelism on top of the M40 wave model. The new
57	`gsd-t parallel` subcommand (owner: m44-d2-parallel-cli) applies a
58	three-gate sequence (D4 depgraph → D5 disjointness → D6 economics)
59	followed by a mode-aware headroom / split gate BEFORE any fan-out.
60	
61	Both thresholds are fixed constants, exported from
62	`bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs`:
63	
64	| Constant | Value | Purpose |
65	|----------|-------|---------|
66	| `IN_SESSION_CW_CEILING_PCT` | 85 | in-session orchestrator-CW ceiling |
67	| `UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT` | 60 | unattended per-worker CW ceiling |
68	| `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_SIZE_PCT` | 4 | heuristic per-worker summary envelope |
69	
70	### [in-session] `computeInSessionHeadroom({ctxPct, workerCount, summarySize})`
71	
72	Returns `{ok, reducedCount}`.
73	
74	- `ok=true, reducedCount=N` iff `ctxPct + N × summarySize ≤ 85` for the
75	  requested worker count N.
76	- If the inequality fails, reduce N and recompute. Final floor is N=1.
77	- **Never returns `ok=false`.** Constraint (m44-d2 constraints.md §Mode
78	  Awareness): in-session mode MUST NEVER throw a pause/resume prompt.
79	  Sequential (N=1) is always feasible because the summary envelope is
80	  spent post-worker, not pre-worker.
81	- When `reducedCount < workerCount`, the caller MUST emit a
82	  `parallelism_reduced` event (schema below).
83	
84	### [unattended] `computeUnattendedGate({estimatedCwPct, threshold=60})`
85	
86	Returns `{ok, split}`.
87	
88	- `ok=true, split=false` iff `estimatedCwPct ≤ threshold`.
89	- `ok=false, split=true` otherwise — caller MUST slice the task into
90	  multiple `claude -p` iters (per-worker CW headroom is the binding
91	  gate for unattended; breaching it would cause mid-run compaction,
92	  which violates the non-negotiable "zero compaction" contract).
93	- Actual task slicing is the orchestrator's responsibility. The gating
94	  function only signals; it emits a `task_split` event and the caller
95	  schedules the split.
96	
97	### Event Schemas
98	
99	All three event types are appended JSONL to
100	`.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` (day-rotated). Event-log failures are
101	best-effort and must never break the caller's control flow.
102	
103	**`parallelism_reduced`** — in-session only:
104	```json
105	{ "type": "parallelism_reduced",
106	  "original_count": 5,
107	  "reduced_count": 3,
108	  "reason": "in_session_headroom",
109	  "ts": "2026-04-23T14:00:00.000Z" }
110	```
111	
112	**`task_split`** — unattended only:
113	```json
114	{ "type": "task_split",
115	  "task_id": "M44-D6-T3",
116	  "estimatedCwPct": 78,
117	  "ts": "2026-04-23T14:00:00.000Z" }
118	```
119	
120	**`gate_veto`** — either mode, emitted when D4 or D5 rejects a task:
121	```json
122	{ "type": "gate_veto",
123	  "task_id": "M44-D2-T4",
124	  "gate": "depgraph" | "disjointness",
125	  "reason": "unmet_deps:M44-D2-T3" | "write-target-overlap-or-unprovable",
126	  "ts": "2026-04-23T14:00:00.000Z" }
127	```
128	
129	A vetoed task falls back to sequential (removed from the parallel
130	batch) but remains in the dry-run plan with decision `veto-deps` or
131	`sequential` so operators can see why.
132	
133	### Invariant Preservation
134	
135	The three pre-existing M40/M44 invariants apply to BOTH modes
136	identically:
137	1. File-disjointness proof BEFORE any parallel spawn (D5 gate)
138	2. 100% automatic merges (M40 orchestrator existing machinery)
139	3. Pre-spawn economics check (D6 estimator)
140	
141	No mode flag bypasses any of these gates.
142	
143	## Versioning
144	- Bump major for changes to the strict-barrier semantics (e.g., speculative next-wave).
145	- Bump minor for new frame types, config keys, or new gating math functions.
146	
147	## Version History
148	
149	- **v1.1.0** (2026-04-23, M44 D2) — Adds §Mode-Aware Gating Math.
150	  Introduces `computeInSessionHeadroom`, `computeUnattendedGate`, the
151	  85% in-session ceiling, the 60% per-worker unattended ceiling, the
152	  4% default summary envelope, and the `parallelism_reduced`,
153	  `task_split`, and `gate_veto` event schemas. Owner: m44-d2-parallel-cli.
154	- **v1.0.0** (M40) — Initial wave-parallelism + ordering semantics.
155
