## Decision Log

> Prior decision log entries preserved in `.gsd-t/milestones/*/progress.md` — see archive snapshots (most recently `M40-external-task-orchestrator-2026-04-20/progress.md`) for pre-M40 history.

- 2026-04-23 16:00: [execute M44-D8 · Wave 3 parallel-with-D2/D3] M44 D8 spawn-plan-visibility complete — 7/7 tasks shipped (T1 writer + contract skeleton `eed80bb` → T2 status-updater + post-commit hook `5a5dae6` → T3 writer integration at 3 chokepoints `83a8227` → T4 plan derivation helper `1a86735` → T5 dashboard `/api/spawn-plans` + SSE channel `42456e3` → T6 transcript right-side panel `d7b1297` → T7 tests + doc-ripple this commit). **Three writers**: `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs::captureSpawn` writes a spawn-plan before `await spawnFn()` and closes it in both success/error paths; `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs::autoSpawnHeadless` writes before the detached child launches; `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` Step 0 writes at every worker iteration under `GSD_T_UNATTENDED_WORKER=1`. All three calls are wrapped in try/catch — plan-write failure NEVER blocks the spawn (constraints §Hard rule 2). **Writer derivation** is a deterministic projection of `.gsd-t/partition.md` + `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md` via `bin/spawn-plan-derive.cjs` — no LLM calls, no prompts, silent-fall to empty-slice when partition absent. **Atomic writes only** (temp file + rename; no partial JSON visible to the reader). **Storage**: `.gsd-t/spawns/{spawnId}.json` schema v1. **Post-commit hook**: `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` + template `templates/hooks/post-commit-spawn-plan.sh` — greps commit message for all `[M\d+-D\d+-T\d+]` ids, scans active plans, sums token attribution from `.gsd-t/token-log.md` rows where `Task` column matches AND `Datetime-start >= spawn.startedAt`, calls `markTaskDone` with `{taskId, commit, tokens}`. Silent-fail: always `exit 0`. **Reader**: new `GET /api/spawn-plans` returns active plans (newest-first); new SSE channel `/api/spawn-plans/stream` uses `fs.watch` on `.gsd-t/spawns/*.json` with mtime-dedup; initial snapshot emitted on connect. `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` adds right-side `<aside class="spawn-panel">` with Layer 1 (project) + Layer 2 (active spawn); status icons `☐ ◐ ✓`; token cells render as `in=12.5k out=1.7k $0.42` (k-suffix above 1000, 2-decimal USD), `—` when attribution null. Grid extended from `280px 1fr` → `280px 1fr 320px`; existing rail is now `aside.left-rail`. **Additive everywhere**: no transcript-stream rendering changes, no token-log header changes, no envelope parse changes, no existing endpoint changes, no existing SSE channel changes. **Hard-rule hook bug caught during tests**: the initial hook inline script used `process.argv[2]` / `process.argv[3]` for positional args, but `node -e <script> A B` places A at argv[1] and B at argv[2]. Fixed in both the canonical script and template. **Contract**: new `.gsd-t/contracts/spawn-plan-contract.md` v1.0.0. **Tests**: 5 new files, 36 tests all green (`test/m44-d8-spawn-plan-writer.test.js` 9 · `test/m44-d8-spawn-plan-status-updater.test.js` 10 · `test/m44-d8-post-commit-hook.test.js` 5 · `test/m44-d8-dashboard-spawn-plans-endpoint.test.js` 5 · `test/m44-d8-transcript-renderer-panel.test.js` 9). Full suite delta +36 (1850→1907 total, 4 pre-existing failures unrelated to D8). **Disjointness with D2**: D8 owns `bin/spawn-plan-*`, `scripts/gsd-t-post-commit-spawn-plan.sh`, `templates/hooks/post-commit-spawn-plan.sh`, `.gsd-t/spawns/`, and adds net-new code blocks to `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` (pre-spawn write + markSpawnEnded), `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs` (pre-spawn write + close path), `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` (2 new routes + helpers), `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html` (new aside + CSS + JS panel), `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` (new bash block under the existing worker-bypass section). D2's config/orchestrator/CLI surface untouched.

- 2026-04-23 15:30: [execute M44-D2 · Wave 3 integration] `gsd-t parallel` subcommand shipped end-to-end in 5 commits (T1 `3d6a809` CLI scaffold + config stubs → T2 `3ca41ff` mode-aware gating math + runParallel wiring → T3 `deff6a6` dry-run plan table + three-gate wiring → T4 `01ea87f` wave-join-contract v1.1.0 + 21 unit tests + D2-surface restore → T5 doc-ripple). **T1 scaffold**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs` (new, exports `runParallel` + `runCli`), extended `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` with `computeInSessionHeadroom`/`computeUnattendedGate` stubs + exported constants, added `parallel` case to `bin/gsd-t.js` dispatcher. `gsd-t parallel --help` verified. **T2 math**: `computeInSessionHeadroom` returns `{ok, reducedCount}` — ok iff `ctxPct + N × 4% ≤ 85%`; reduces N until floor of 1 (NEVER refuses, per in-session contract). `computeUnattendedGate` returns `{ok, split}` — split when `estimatedCwPct > 60`. `runParallel` wires D4 depgraph → D5 disjointness → D6 economics sequentially; in-session reads `ctxPct` from `token-budget.getSessionStatus()` and emits `parallelism_reduced` on reduction; unattended per-task calls D6 estimator and emits `task_split` on > 60%. Gate vetoes (D4 unmet deps, D5 write-target overlap) emit `gate_veto` events; all events best-effort JSONL to `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl`. **T3 dry-run**: 6-column plan table (`task_id | domain | estimated CW% | disjoint? | deps ok? | decision`) + `Total workers: N   Mode: …` trailer; `--mode` both space and `=` forms; `--milestone`, `--domain`, `--dry-run`, `--help` flags. Auto-detect fallback: `GSD_T_UNATTENDED=1` → unattended, else in-session; explicit `--mode` overrides env. **T4 contract + tests**: bumped `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 with new §Mode-Aware Gating Math documenting both thresholds (85 in-session, 60 unattended), the 4% summary envelope, all three event schemas (`parallelism_reduced`, `task_split`, `gate_veto`), the `reducedCount` floor behavior, and invariant preservation. Added Version History section. `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js` (new, 21 tests): headroom ok/reduced/floor/boundary, unattended gate ok/split/boundary/custom threshold, `formatPlanTable` column order + rendering, `runParallel` two-task parallel + disjointness fallback + mode auto-detect + 70% ctxPct reduction end-to-end, CLI arg parsing. **Also restored D2's owned surfaces**: commit `83a8227` (D8-T3) inadvertently reverted D2's config-module additions and the `parallel` case in `bin/gsd-t.js` (D8's partition scope was `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` + headless/dashboard/transcript only — the config revert was out of scope). T4 re-lands D2's owned code alongside the v1.1.0 contract bump; D8's own changes remain untouched. **T5 doc-ripple**: `docs/requirements.md` new §"M44 Parallel CLI (D2 — Wave 3 integration, 2026-04-23)" with 10 REQ entries (REQ-M44-D2-01 through REQ-M44-D2-10); `docs/architecture.md` new §"Parallel CLI (M44 D2, v3.18.10+)" with dispatch-flow diagram, mode contracts table, integration note (extends, does not replace M40), event schemas, hard invariants, contract + test pointers; `README.md` adds parallel CLI block under CLI Commands (examples + contract reference). `GSD-T-README.md` confirmed absent (per 2026-04-21 21:10 Decision Log) — no update. **Tests**: 21/21 in new file, 15/15 still pass in `m40-orchestrator-config.test.js`; full suite delta +21 new tests. Pre-existing failures (3) are unrelated: `event-stream.test.js`, `m43-dashboard-autostart.test.js`, `watch-progress-writer.test.js` — same 3 failures observed at f5e547a baseline. **D2 files owned and committed**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs` (new), `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` (modified), `bin/gsd-t.js` (subcommand dispatch), `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js` (new), `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md` (v1.1.0), plus T5 ripple. Next in Wave 3: D3 (command-file integration — edits `commands/gsd-t-{execute,wave,quick,debug,integrate}.md` to dispatch via `gsd-t parallel`) can now start; D8 continues in parallel.

- 2026-04-23 00:10: [execute M44 · unattended Wave 1 worker iter 2] Wave 1 closeout — D7 T4 test file (`test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js`, 19 tests) committed; D7 T5 doc-ripple already landed in-tree (requirements + architecture + progress log) now committed alongside; D1 T1-T5 and D7 T1-T5 task status flipped from `[ ] pending` to `[x] done (· commit `XXX`)` in both `tasks.md` files so the task-graph reader now reports them as satisfied deps for Wave 2. **Fix landed in same commit**: `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` top-level stdin wiring wrapped in `require.main === module` guard. Root cause: when the hook is `require()`d by a test file (as `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` does to exercise the `handle()` export directly), the module-level `process.stdin.setEncoding('utf8')` + `stdin.on('data'/'end')` listeners attach to the *test process's* stdin and keep the Node event loop alive indefinitely. All 19 D7 assertions pass in ~400ms isolated, but in the `npm test` full-suite run the file hangs on the unclosed stdin handle until the test runner kills it at its internal timeout (54885ms observed), which flagged the file as `✖` even though every sub-test was green. The `require.main === module` guard is the standard Node pattern for "only run top-level code when invoked as a script" and is functionally invisible in production (Claude Code invokes hooks via `node scripts/…` = `require.main === module` is true). After fix: 69/69 pass across D1 + D7 + m41-token-capture + compact-detector in 566ms combined. **Wave 1 gate status**: D1 DAG + D7 `cw_id` field + calibration hook all land; `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` new rows carry `cw_id` when supplied; `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` can now receive `compaction_post_spawn` calibration rows. Wave 2 (D4 + D5 + D6, all parallel-safe) unblocked.

- 2026-04-22 23:00: [execute M44-D7 · unattended Wave 1] M44 D7 per-CW attribution complete — 5/5 tasks shipped in sequence (T1 metrics-schema v2.1.0 + compaction-events v1.1.0 contract bumps `7c3e571` → T2 cw_id pass-through in `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` `3d784ed` → T3 calibration hook `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` `64301ed` → T4 unit test suite `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` 19/19 isolated → T5 doc-ripple this commit). **Token-capture wrapper** — `recordSpawnRow` and `captureSpawn` now accept optional `cw_id` opt; `_buildJsonlRecord` serializes it only when supplied (`if (cw_id != null && cw_id !== '') rec.cw_id = String(cw_id);` — same pattern as the existing `session_id` / `turn_id` branches, so pre-D7 callers produce byte-identical rows). 15/15 existing `m41-token-capture.test.js` tests still pass with no modifications. **Calibration hook** — new SessionStart handler that runs alongside (not replacing) `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js`. On `payload.source === "compact"` it reads `.gsd-t/.unattended/state.json`, derives `spawn_id` (sessionId fallback chain), `cw_id` (= spawn_id for unattended workers), `task_id` (activeTask / task_id / currentTask fallback chain), `estimatedCwPct` (D6's pre-spawn prediction if recorded), `cwCeilingTokens` (defaults to 200000), then derives `actualCwPct` from `payload.input_tokens` (or nested `compactMetadata.preTokens`) divided by the ceiling, clamped to [0, 2.0]. Appends one `{type: "compaction_post_spawn", schemaVersion: 1, ts, cw_id, task_id, spawn_id, estimatedCwPct, actualCwPct}` row to `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl`. **Silent no-op** when payload isn't a compact source, when `.gsd-t/` is missing, when `state.json` is missing/unparseable/`status !== "running"`, when no `spawn_id` derivable, when no `input_tokens` derivable, or when `cwd` is non-absolute. Always exits 0 (throwing breaks Claude Code SessionStart). Zero ext deps, 1 MiB stdin cap, path-traversal guard mirroring the detector. **Coexistence verified by test**: pre-existing v1.0.0 detector rows (`source: "compact"`, no `type` field) remain intact when calibration row appended; consumers distinguish via `type` field (`"compaction_post_spawn"` vs absent). **Contracts bumped** in this domain (per constraints — must land in D7's commit, not Wave 2): `metrics-schema-contract.md` v2 → v2.1.0 (adds optional `cw_id` field with derivation rules — unattended: `cw_id == spawn_id`; in-session: `session_id + ":" + compaction_index` per the calibration hook's compact-fire counter), `compaction-events-contract.md` v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 (adds `compaction_post_spawn` event type, calibration hook lifecycle, guardrails, three-hook wiring matrix). **Test suite**: `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` — 19 tests, all green isolated: cw_id present → field serialized, cw_id numeric → coerced to string, cw_id absent/null/empty → field OMITTED (not null, not ""), captureSpawn forwards cw_id, `_buildJsonlRecord` cw_id branch matches session_id pattern, calibration hook with active spawn → calibration row written with correct task_id + estimatedCwPct + actualCwPct ~0.97 for 194000/200000, fallback to compactMetadata.preTokens, cwCeilingTokens override (95000/100000 = 0.95), no state.json → no row written, status !== running → no-op, malformed json → silent, source !== compact → no-op, missing input_tokens → no-op, empty/non-json stdin → no-op, non-absolute cwd → no-op, calibration row appends alongside v1.0.0 detector rows in same sink, zero ext deps. **Hard constraints all met** (per `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d7-per-cw-attribution/constraints.md`): backward-compatible cw_id (existing callers unchanged, absent ≠ null), silent no-op when no active spawn, zero ext deps, calibration event includes BOTH estimatedCwPct AND actualCwPct, contract bumps in D7's commit, `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` NOT modified. **Out of scope** (deferred): backfilling pre-D7 token-usage.jsonl rows with cw_id (525 historical rows; D6 falls back to per-iter median); economics logic (D6); parallel dispatch (D2); CW boundary detection algorithm beyond the spawn_id == cw_id rule. **D1 ran in parallel** (also Wave 1 Foundation per partition map: D1 + D7 both no-dep). Files: `bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs` (cw_id opt + serialization branch), `scripts/gsd-t-calibration-hook.js` (new, 254 lines), `.gsd-t/contracts/metrics-schema-contract.md` (v2.1.0 §added), `.gsd-t/contracts/compaction-events-contract.md` (v1.1.0 §added), `test/m44-cw-attribution.test.js` (new, 19 tests), `docs/requirements.md` (+§"M44 Per-CW Attribution" 6 REQs), `docs/architecture.md` (+§"Per-CW Attribution (M44 D7)" with diagram, hook lifecycle, contract pointers), this entry.

- 2026-04-22 16:30: [execute M44-D1 · unattended Wave 1] M44 D1 task-graph reader complete — 5/5 tasks shipped in sequence (T1 contract skeleton + module scaffold `26979df` → T2 real parser + cycle detection + scope.md fallback `57076d6` → T3 unit test suite 22/22 pass `b799e83` → T4 `gsd-t graph --output json|table` CLI `f5ee283` → T5 doc-ripple this commit). New `bin/gsd-t-task-graph.cjs` (305 lines, zero ext deps): `buildTaskGraph({projectDir})` returns `{nodes, edges, ready, byId, warnings}` after parsing every `.gsd-t/domains/*/tasks.md`; node schema `{id, domain, wave, title, status, deps, touches}`; iterative three-color DFS cycle detection throws `TaskGraphCycleError` with `.cycle: string[]`; touch-list resolution prefers task `**Touches**` / `**Files touched**`, falls back to `scope.md ## Files Owned`, falls back to `[]` + warning; status markers `[ ]/[x]/[-]/[!]` + unknown → pending + warning; only `done` deps satisfy dependents (skipped/failed do NOT). New contract `.gsd-t/contracts/task-graph-contract.md` v0.1.0 (T1 skeleton) → v1.0.0 (T2 final, downstream M44 domains may now begin implementation). New test suite `test/m44-task-graph.test.js` — 22/22 pass in 51 ms isolated, covering single-domain, multi-domain cross-domain, 3-task ring + self-loop cycle, ready-mask done/skipped/failed/unknown-dep semantics, scope.md fallback, [] fallback + warning, unknown status marker warning, parseDepList/parseFileList edge cases, missing domains dir, getReadyTasks null-safety, perf (50 domains × 5 tasks = 250 nodes parses in ~6 ms vs 200 ms budget), live-repo smoke. CLI surface in `bin/gsd-t.js doGraph`: `--output json` and `--output table` (id/domain/wave/status/ready/deps); also explicit `gsd-t graph tasks [json|table]`; pre-existing `graph index|status|query` (codebase entity graph) untouched. Live verified on this repo: 33 tasks · 36 edges · 2 ready (M44-D1-T1 + M44-D7-T1, the two no-dep Wave-1 foundation tasks) · 0 warnings · 3 ms parse. Doc-ripple landed: `docs/requirements.md` §"M44 Task-Graph Reader" with 7 REQs (REQ-M44-D1-01..07); `docs/architecture.md` §"Task-Graph Reader (M44 D1)" with consumer diagram, public API, hard rules, CLI surface. **Hard constraints all met**: zero ext deps (Node built-ins only), cycle detection mandatory + non-recoverable, read-only at all times, mode-agnostic (no in-session vs unattended awareness), perf < 200 ms (3 ms live / 6 ms synthetic), contract gate (v1.0.0 locks schema for D2/D4/D5/D6 to start). D7 running in parallel (Wave 1 Foundation per partition map: D1 + D7 both no-dep). Files: `bin/gsd-t-task-graph.cjs` (new), `test/m44-task-graph.test.js` (new), `.gsd-t/contracts/task-graph-contract.md` (new), `bin/gsd-t.js` (+graph subcommand routing), `docs/requirements.md` (+§M44 Task-Graph Reader), `docs/architecture.md` (+§Task-Graph Reader (M44 D1)), `.gsd-t/progress.md` (this entry).

- 2026-04-22 22:31: [partition M44 · unattended iter 1] M44 partitioned into 7 domains across 3 waves (Foundation: D1+D7 → Gates: D4+D5+D6 → Integration: D2→D3). Mode-aware gating math layered on M40 orchestrator (built-on, not replaced). Shared-file conflict map identifies orchestrator.js + orchestrator-config.cjs as D2/D6 split, command files as D3-owned, token-capture.cjs as D7 cw_id pass-through. Integration points: post-Wave-2 D6 estimator vs D7-tagged real slice + D4+D5 synthetic 2-task fixture; post-Wave-3 in-session multi-domain smoke (assert ≤T/2 + zero pause prompts) + unattended 5-iter chain over multi-task milestone (assert zero compactions in compactions.jsonl). Pre-reqs all landed (Q1/Q2a/Q2b/Q3, optimization report, adaptive maxParallel). No known blockers. Calibration corpus: 525 turn rows + 72 compaction events. Next: /gsd-t-plan for M44 to populate per-domain tasks.md task bodies (or proceed straight to /gsd-t-execute Wave 1 if tasks.md already concrete).

- 2026-04-22 23:55: [quick] transcript renderer — per-frame arrival timestamp pill. User feedback during M44 unattended run: *"Each message and tool call should include a time stamp so I can easily see from the stream if something got stuck."* Added a small monospace HH:MM:SS pill (with ISO `title` tooltip) prepended to every frame in `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`. Single chokepoint: `appendFrame(el, arrivedAt)` now inserts a `<span class="ts">` as the first child of every frame div. SSE `onmessage` captures `arrivedAt = new Date()` once per message and threads it through `renderFrame` → all 9 per-type render helpers (`renderSystem`, `renderUserMessage`, `renderAssistantText`, `renderThinking`, `renderToolUse`, `renderToolResult`, `renderBoundary`, `renderRaw`, `renderCompactMarker`). Stuck-stream detection is now visual: two adjacent frames with identical HH:MM:SS = nothing arrived between them; a long gap before a new timestamp = the stream paused. Tests: `test/m44-transcript-timestamp.test.js` — 7 asserts (CSS rule present + appendFrame signature + fmtTs/pad2 helpers + SSE captures + renderFrame threading + script syntactically valid + sandboxed-eval unit test of `fmtTs(Date)` → `"09:05:07"`). All 14 transcript renderer tests pass (7 new + 7 prior). Renderer-only change — no contract, domain, or server impact.

- 2026-04-22 23:30: [quick] supervisor self-healing — stream-json spawn args + JSON-or-integer pid parser. Two bugs surfaced during the M44 unattended launch: (1) supervisor's explicit `args:` to `claude -p` omitted `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, so the worker buffered all output until exit and the M42 transcript NDJSON stayed at 0 bytes mid-iter; (2) `supervisor.pid` is now JSON `{pid, projectDir, startedAt}` (per `bin/supervisor-pid-fingerprint.cjs writePidFile`) but three command-file readers parsed it with `parseInt(...trim(), 10)` — getting `NaN`, treating the supervisor as dead while it was running fine. Fix scope: spawn-args added to both the supervisor's explicit args (`bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs:1381`) and the platform default fallback (`bin/gsd-t-unattended-platform.cjs:154`); PID readers in `commands/gsd-t-unattended-watch.md` (Step 2 PID branch), `commands/gsd-t-unattended.md` (Step 1b pre-flight + Step 3 liveness loop) now require the canonical `readPidFile()` from `bin/supervisor-pid-fingerprint.cjs` which transparently handles both JSON and legacy bare-integer forms. `commands/gsd-t-resume.md` already used `readPidFile()` — no change needed. Two new regression tests: `test/m44-supervisor-stream-json-args.test.js` (3 asserts: explicit-args site + default fallback both contain `--output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions`) and `test/m44-pid-parser-canonical.test.js` (6 asserts across three command files: no `parseInt(readFileSync(PID_FILE))` pattern + each uses `readPidFile`; plus a unit test that `readPidFile` returns `{form:"json"}` and `{form:"legacy"}` correctly). Full suite **1767/1769** — exactly the prior 1758/1760 baseline plus 9 new tests. Same 2 unrelated pre-existing failures (flaky dashboard port test + M43-progress-md hardcoded expectation now stale post-M44 promotion). This commit is shipped per the user's standing self-healing directive: *"if you see a problem and you've identified a fix for it, just stop unattended, implement your fix, and restart it without asking me."* Files changed: `bin/gsd-t-unattended.cjs`, `bin/gsd-t-unattended-platform.cjs`, `commands/gsd-t-unattended-watch.md`, `commands/gsd-t-unattended.md`, `test/m44-supervisor-stream-json-args.test.js` (new), `test/m44-pid-parser-canonical.test.js` (new). Next: relaunch `/gsd-t-unattended M44`.

Extended `scripts/gsd-t-compact-detector.js` with a second write path: after appending the compactions.jsonl row, the hook now also appends a `{type:"compact_marker", ts, source, session_id, prior_session_id, trigger?, preTokens?, postTokens?}` frame to the most-recently-modified `.ndjson` in `<cwd>/.gsd-t/transcripts/`. Active transcript discovery (`findActiveTranscript`) reads the transcripts dir, filters `*.ndjson`, picks the file with the highest mtime — no-ops silently when the dir is absent or empty. Identical guardrails as the Q2a write: path-traversal guard (resolved transcript path must stay under `.gsd-t/transcripts/`), off-switch (no `.gsd-t/` → silent no-op), fail-open (transcript append failure swallowed so compactions.jsonl write always completes). Updated `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`: new CSS `.frame.compact-marker` (orange/yellow horizontal rule with centered badge), new `renderCompactMarker(frame)` function (exports as `window.__gsdtRenderCompactMarker`), badge label "⚡ CW boundary", tooltip = `ts + trigger + prior_session_id + session_id + preTokens + postTokens` — only fields present in the frame. `renderFrame()` dispatches `type === "compact_marker"` before all other frame types. New `test/m44-compact-marker-frame.test.js` — **11/11 pass**: schema required fields; optional fields included/omitted correctly; appends without overwriting prior content; most-recently-modified transcript chosen; no-op when `.gsd-t/` absent; no-op when transcripts dir absent; no-op when dir has no `.ndjson`; hook exits 0 even when transcript write fails (compactions.jsonl still written); renderer exports `renderCompactMarker`; badge has `cm-badge` class + "CW boundary" text + tooltip with trigger/tokens; no throw on legacy frame. - 2026-04-22 22:15: [backlog] M44 entry added to `.gsd-t/backlog.md` (#14) with mode-aware framing — **in-session and unattended are equally first-class** delivery targets, not primary + secondary. The continue-here file from earlier in the session claimed M44 was already in the backlog — it was not; grep came back empty. Now landed. Objectives per mode: [in-session] speed + minimize-compaction (hard rule: NEVER throw interactive pause/resume prompts); [unattended] zero compaction across autonomous M1→M10 run. Three pre-existing invariants (file-disjointness, 100% auto-merges, pre-spawn economics) apply to both modes identically — they're correctness, not mode. Mode-specific gating math: [in-session] orchestrator-CW headroom (worker summaries land in user-facing CW; bound fan-out width AND result-envelope size) vs [unattended] per-worker-CW headroom (supervisor is Node process with no CW; bind per-`claude -p` worker CW ceiling). Two delivery layers (L1: parallel `claude -p` worker spawns, L2: parallel tasks within one worker) are mode-agnostic. **Token-cost honesty in the entry**: parallel is approximately token-neutral in steady state (total input/output same, wall-clock is the primary win; `max` instead of `sum`), and token-positive only in the case where sequential would have compacted (compaction-induced re-input drops to zero). Rate-limit pressure is explicitly NOT a con — hitting the 5-hour ceiling because 20× more work got done is the goal. 7 proposed domains (D1 task-graph reader, D2 `gsd-t parallel` CLI, D3 command-file integration, D4 dep-graph validation, D5 file-disjointness prover, D6 pre-spawn economics + cost estimator consuming the 525-row calibration corpus, D7 per-CW attribution + compaction-event integration). Pre-reqs already landed this session: Q1 token-log regen (`7eefd2c`), Q2a compaction detector + scanner (`940e5a8` + `f7de324`), Q3 turn→tool join (`8f4588b`), optimization report (`b5edff2`), adaptive maxParallel (`969462a`). Companion memory `feedback_parallelism_two_modes.md` written; prior `feedback_unattended_overnight_only.md` superseded — time-of-day framing retired, mode is now chosen by *intent* (interrupt-ability vs zero-touch autonomy) not clock. User framing that drove this: "In session, there's two clear objectives, speed and reduce compaction as much as is possible" + "Unattended must run M1 → M10 end-to-end with zero human involvement and zero compaction. This is critical, but not for all builds. Many I will want to run in session because I don't have all the requirements defined or I want to be able to interrupt." Success criteria: [in-session] T/2 wall-clock reduction with N workers and zero pause prompts; [unattended] zero compaction events logged during a measured autonomous chain (baseline: 72 compactions over 18 days of mixed-mode work from Q2a backfill).

- 2026-04-22 21:50: [quick] token-usage optimization report generator — `gsd-t report tokens [--date YYYY-MM-DD] [--out PATH]` writes a markdown report at `.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-{DATE}.md` organized per the canonical 5-level hierarchy `Run → Iter → CW → Turn → Tool`, with CW (context window) as the primary optimization unit (peer of Iter, not a partition of it). New `bin/gsd-t-report-tokens.cjs` (430 lines) exports `generateReport`, `groupIntoCWs`, `rollupCW`, `topNExpensiveTurns`, `groupCompactionEvents`, `renderMarkdown`. CW grouping rule: each distinct `session_id` in `.gsd-t/metrics/token-usage.jsonl` = one CW; compactions in `.gsd-t/metrics/compactions.jsonl` whose `prior_session_id` matches a CW mark `endedBy: compaction (auto|manual)` (manual when `trigger=manual`, auto otherwise; missing trigger defaults to auto with a footnote dagger). CWs without a following compaction get `iter end` (if a later CW exists) or `run end` (if it's the last in the data). Live + backfill compaction rows are deduped by `(ts, session_id, prior_session_id)` with live `source=compact` winning over `compact-backfill` when both exist. Report sections: **A** per-CW rollup (PRIMARY) — `CW# | Iter | Start | Turns | In | Out | CacheR | CacheC | Avg Out/turn | Peak Ctx% | Ended-by` with totals + average-turns-per-CW + compaction rate; **B** tool-tokens rollup via `bin/gsd-t-tool-attribution.cjs::joinTurnsAndEvents` + `aggregateByTool` — B.1 by-tool table + B.2 tool×command cross-tab (top 10 tools × top 5 commands by total tokens); **C** top-20 expensive turns sorted by `input + output` descending (input-heavy = context bloat, output-heavy = generation cost — both columns visible); **D** all compaction events with `ts | source | iter | trigger | preTokens | postTokens | durationMs | active command | active domain/task` enriched with the last turn's command/domain/task before the compaction `ts`. **No cost columns anywhere** (Max subscription = $0); **idempotent** (same date overwrites same path; output deterministic modulo the `Generated:` ISO line); **never crashes on missing data** (missing `compactions.jsonl` → "no compaction events recorded"; failed tool attribution → "Tool attribution unavailable"). CLI dispatch: new `case "report"` block in `bin/gsd-t.js` introduces nested-subcommand pattern (`gsd-t report tokens …`); help text bumped. Tests: new `test/m44-report-tokens.test.js` — **13/13 pass**: groupIntoCWs (0/2 compactions, live+backfill dedup, missing-trigger flag), rollupCW (sums + avg + peak + null ctxPct), topNExpensiveTurns (≥N + <N), groupCompactionEvents activity enrichment, generateReport end-to-end (4 sections + correct CW count + run-end + missing-compactions survival + idempotence). Full suite **1748/1749 pass** — 1 fail is pre-existing unrelated (`m43-milestone-complete-detection` asserts on live `progress.md` content — stale fixture). **Live report on this repo** (`.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md`): 1 CW with all 523 turn rows of the current session_id, top tool `no-tool` (285 of 286 attributed turns landed there because most turns have zero matched tool events) followed by Bash/Edit/Read/ScheduleWakeup. Section D shows the full 72-event compaction history (61 auto + 11 manual, all backfill) — the historical record M44's pre-req scanner produced. **Surprise**: only 1 CW in the per-CW rollup for the entire dataset because every token-usage row shares one session_id (`a5ee3b8e-1a77`); the 72 compactions are all _ancestor_ events (their `prior_session_id` doesn't match any in our turn set), so they show only in Section D and not as CW boundaries. This is correct given the data shape (all 523 rows landed in one Stop-hook capture window). V2 deferred: roll up across `.gsd-t/transcripts/s-*.ndjson` `result` envelopes for run-level (cross-session) totals; HTML/dashboard widget; per-CW tool breakdown sub-table. Red Team skipped per quick spec — V1 analyst tool, not user-facing critical path. Files: `bin/gsd-t-report-tokens.cjs` (new, 430 lines), `bin/gsd-t.js` (+59 lines: report dispatch + help line), `test/m44-report-tokens.test.js` (new, 280 lines), `.gsd-t/reports/token-usage-2026-04-22.md` (live output, 156 lines).

- 2026-04-22 21:30: [quick] orchestrator default `--max-parallel` is now adaptive — `floor(freemem / 2GB)` clamped to floor 3, ceiling 15. Reverts the static `10` from `67c520a` which was calibrated for the maintainer's high-RAM Mac and would risk OOM on smaller machines (10 × ~1.5–2 GB resident per `claude -p` worker). Static `DEFAULTS.maxParallel` drops back to `3` (the safe floor). New `computeAdaptiveMaxParallel(freeBytes)` in `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` is invoked when no project `orchestrator.config.json` sets `maxParallel`, no `--max-parallel` flag, and no `GSD_T_MAX_PARALLEL` env. Precedence chain: `default(3) < adaptive(floor(free/2GB), [3..15]) < config-file < CLI < env`. New `loadConfig` return field `maxParallelSource` exposes which level decided ('adaptive' | 'config-file' | 'cli' | 'env') for observability. Help text updated; wave-join contract updated to "default: adaptive — floor(freemem / 2GB) clamped to floor 3 / ceiling 15". Tests: 4 new in `test/m40-orchestrator-config.test.js` covering adaptive math at 0/1/4/8/16/20/64 GB free + NaN guard, plus a regression test asserting `DEFAULTS.maxParallel === 3` (the safe floor). All 15/15 config tests + 58/58 across the full M40 orchestrator suite pass. Existing `.gsd-t/orchestrator.config.json` overrides still take precedence — power users (incl. this maintainer) can pin `maxParallel: 10` per-project without touching defaults.

# Tasks: m44-d2-parallel-cli

## Wave 3 — Integration (D2 runs first within Wave 3)

### M44-D2-T1 — CLI scaffold + mode-aware config extension skeleton
- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit 3d6a809)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D1-T5 (D1 complete), M44-D4-T4 (D4 complete), M44-D5-T4 (D5 complete), M44-D6-T5 (D6 complete), M44-D7-T5 (D7 complete)
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs` file exists, exports `runParallel({projectDir, mode, milestone, domain, dryRun})`
  - `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` extended with stubs for `computeInSessionHeadroom` and `computeUnattendedGate` (implementation in T2)
  - `gsd-t parallel --help` prints usage without crashing
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs` (new), `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs` (modified), `bin/gsd-t.js` (add `parallel` subcommand routing)

### M44-D2-T2 — Mode-aware gating math implementation
- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit 3ca41ff)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D2-T1
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `computeInSessionHeadroom({ctxPct, workerCount, summarySize})` returns `{ok, reducedCount}` — `ok=true` if `ctxPct + workerCount × summarySize ≤ 85`, else suggests reduced count until N=1
  - `computeUnattendedGate({estimatedCwPct, threshold=60})` returns `{ok, split}` — if `estimatedCwPct > threshold`, `split=true` and the caller must slice the task into multiple iters
  - [in-session] path: `runParallel` calls `computeInSessionHeadroom` before fan-out; if reduced, logs the reduction decision to `.gsd-t/events/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl` as a `parallelism_reduced` event
  - [unattended] path: `runParallel` calls D6 estimator + `computeUnattendedGate` before spawning; if split required, emits a `task_split` event and spawns multiple iters
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs`, `bin/gsd-t-orchestrator-config.cjs`

### M44-D2-T3 — `--dry-run` output + end-to-end gate wiring
- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit <pending>)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D2-T2
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `gsd-t parallel --dry-run` prints: proposed worker plan table (task id | domain | estimated CW% | disjoint? | deps ok? | decision), total worker count, mode used
  - Three gates wired: D4 dep-validation, D5 disjointness prover, D6 economics all called in sequence; any gate veto logs a `gate_veto` event and falls back to sequential for the affected task pair
  - `--mode in-session` and `--mode unattended` explicitly tested against the synthetic 2-task fixture
- **Files touched**: `bin/gsd-t-parallel.cjs`

### M44-D2-T4 — wave-join-contract bump + unit tests
- **Status**: [x] done (2026-04-23 · commit 01ea87f)
- **Dependencies**: M44-D2-T3
- **Acceptance criteria**:
  - `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md` bumped to v1.1.0 with §"Mode-Aware Gating Math" section describing both mode paths, thresholds, and fallback behavior
  - `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js` covers: in-session headroom computation (ok and reduced cases), unattended gate (ok and split cases), dry-run output format, gate veto fallback
  - All tests pass via `npm test`
- **Files touched**: `.gsd-t/contracts/wave-join-contract.md`, `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js` (new)

 - 2026-04-23 00:25: [graft M44 · D8 spawn-plan-visibility added to partition] New domain M44-D8 grafted into M44 in flight. User request 2026-04-23: right-side two-layer task panel in dashboard / transcript visualizer (Layer 1 project tasks, Layer 2 active spawn tasks) + per-task token cell on done items. Design fork chosen: explicit plan file (option B), one protocol with three writers (captureSpawn, autoSpawnHeadless, unattended worker resume Step 0) and one reader (dashboard SSE + transcript HTML panel). Plan-file schema at `.gsd-t/spawns/{spawnId}.json` includes `tokens: {in, out, cr, cc, cost_usd}` field per task; post-commit hook writes attribution from `.gsd-t/token-log.md` when status flips to `done`. Token cell format: `in=Nk out=Nk $X.XX` right-aligned monospace; `—` when null (per CLAUDE.md "zero is a measurement, dash is acknowledged gap"). Status icons: `☐ ◐ ✓`, only one `◐` per spawn at a time. Wave assignment: D8 lands in Wave 3 fully parallel-safe with D2 and D3 — disjoint write targets, additive-only edits to 5 existing files (`bin/gsd-t-token-capture.cjs`, `bin/headless-auto-spawn.cjs`, `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js`, `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, `commands/gsd-t-resume.md`). Hard constraints locked in `constraints.md`: writer derives-never-decides, spawn launches even if writer fails (try/catch at all 3 chokepoints), atomic writes only, post-commit hook silent-fail, no new LLM token cost, no transcript-derivation fallback (per user: no half-measures). 7 tasks (T1..T7) defined. Token cost per spawn: ~0 added (writer is pure I/O on files the spawn was going to read anyway; reader is browser-only; status updater is shell+node). Files added: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d8-spawn-plan-visibility/{scope,tasks,constraints}.md`. Files edited: `.gsd-t/partition.md` (domains 7→8, Wave 3 row added, Shared Files & Conflict Map updated, Execution Order updated). Ready for Wave 3 execute.
 
+- 2026-04-23 00:50: [graft M44 · D9 parallelism-observability added to partition] New domain M44-D9 grafted into M44. User request 2026-04-23: third dashboard panel showing live parallelism state (active workers, ready tasks, parallelism factor, gate decision tally) with color-coded health (green/yellow/red) — RED when orchestrator failing to fan out OR wave parallelism factor < 50% of D6 estimate. Full Report button dumps markdown post-mortem (per-spawn table, per-gate decisions, Gantt-ish per-worker timeline, token cost vs. D6 estimate). Wave assignment: D9 lands in Wave 3, parallel-safe with D2/D3/D8 — sequenced AFTER D8-T1 (reader requires spawn-plan files to exist). 4 tasks (T1 module+contract, T2 dashboard endpoints, T3 transcript renderer panel, T4 tests+doc ripple). Hard constraints: reader-never-writer, silent-fail on malformed inputs, no new LLM token cost, dashboard endpoints additive (disjoint from D8's /api/spawn-plans), Stop Supervisor button reuses existing /gsd-t-unattended-stop. Token cost per spawn: ~0 added (pure file I/O). Files added: `.gsd-t/domains/m44-d9-parallelism-observability/{scope,tasks,constraints}.md`. Files edited: `.gsd-t/partition.md` (domains 8→9, Wave 3 row, Shared Files & Conflict Map split for `scripts/gsd-t-dashboard-server.js` + `scripts/gsd-t-transcript.html`, new entries for `commands/gsd-t-help.md` and `docs/architecture.md`). Stop sentinel created to drain supervisor before relaunch with D9 in scope.
+
 - 2026-04-23 17:43: [M44-D2-T5] parallel-cli doc ripple — requirements/architecture/README/GSD-T-README updated; Wave 3 D2 gate met. `docs/requirements.md` §"M44 Parallel CLI" lists 10 REQs (REQ-M44-D2-01..10) covering runParallel surface, orchestrator-config extension (`computeInSessionHeadroom`, `computeUnattendedGate`, `IN_SESSION_CW_CEILING_PCT=85`, `UNATTENDED_PER_WORKER_CW_PCT=60`, `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_SIZE_PCT=4`), three-gate wiring (D4→D5→D6), in-session reduction to N=1 floor (never refuses), unattended task_split on >60% per-worker CW, mode selection (`--mode` flag > `GSD_T_UNATTENDED=1` env > default in-session), `--dry-run` 6-col plan table format, and the `wave-join-contract.md` v1.1.0 bump. `docs/architecture.md` §"Parallel CLI (M44 D2, v3.18.10+)" adds the dataflow diagram (D1 buildTaskGraph → D4 validateDepGraph → D5 proveDisjointness → D6 estimateTaskFootprint × N → mode-aware gating math → plan), mode-contract table, event schemas (`gate_veto`, `parallelism_reduced`, `task_split`), and hard invariants (zero ext deps; M40 orchestrator never replaced; 3 pre-existing invariants apply to BOTH modes; in-session never refuses — N=1 floor; adaptive maxParallel untouched). `README.md` adds `gsd-t parallel` block under Headless Mode with 5 usage examples. `docs/GSD-T-README.md` adds "Parallel CLI (M44)" section with flags table, three upstream gates list, and mode-aware final gate table. Wave 3 D2 gate verified: 21/21 tests pass in `test/m44-parallel-cli.test.js`; full repo suite 1868/1871 (3 pre-existing unrelated fails: event-stream:341, m43-milestone-complete-detection:137, watch-progress-writer:222). D3 can now begin. D2 commits: T1 3d6a809, T2 3ca41ff, T3 deff6a6 (pending→will retro-fix), T4 01ea87f, T5 <this-commit>.
